Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and dependable Data Entry Clerk to join our team. In this role, you will play an important part in keeping our records accurate, organized, and up to date. If you enjoy working with information, have a strong eye for detail, and take pride in producing accurate work, we'd love to hear from you.

The ideal candidate is someone who values accuracy, works well independently, and understands the importance of handling sensitive information with professionalism and care.

Key Responsibilities

Enter, update, and maintain data in company databases and systems.
Review information for accuracy and completeness before inputting records.
Verify and correct data discrepancies when needed.
Organize and maintain digital and physical files.
Generate reports and retrieve information as requested by team members.
Ensure confidential information is handled securely and in accordance with company policies.
Collaborate with colleagues across departments to gather and update information.
Perform routine quality checks to maintain data integrity.
Assist with administrative tasks and other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent required.
Previous data entry, administrative, or clerical experience is preferred.
Strong typing skills with a high level of accuracy.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Word.
Familiarity with database systems and data management tools is an advantage.
Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.
Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.

What We're Looking For

A reliable and trustworthy individual who takes ownership of their work.
Someone who enjoys working with details and maintaining organized records.
A team player who is willing to help others and contribute to a positive work environment.
A self-motivated professional who can work independently while staying focused on quality.

Why Join Us?

We believe every role contributes to the success of our organization. As a Data Entry Clerk, your work will help ensure that important information remains accurate, accessible, and reliable for teams across the company. We offer a supportive workplace, opportunities to learn and grow, and a culture that values respect, collaboration, and attention to detail.

Title: Data Analyst, Governance
Description:
About The Company

Progressive Leasing is a leading provider of in-store and e-commerce lease-to-own solutions. With more than 20 years of experience in the FinTech industry, we have established ourselves as an innovative and customer-centric organization. Our commitment to simplifying the leasing process and valuing people has driven our growth from a start-up to an industry leader. As a subsidiary of PROG Holdings (NYSE: PRG), we operate within a diversified FinTech holding company that includes three distinct business segments: Progressive Leasing, Purchasing Power—an employee purchase program utilizing payroll deduction for consumer products and services—and Four, a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) platform. Our mission is to empower consumers and retail partners through innovative financial solutions, ensuring accessible and flexible leasing options that meet evolving market needs.

About The Role

We are currently seeking a highly skilled Lead Data Governance Analyst to join our dynamic team. This role is pivotal in driving our data governance initiatives and ensuring the integrity, security, and effective use of data across the organization. The position offers the flexibility of working remotely from anywhere in the continental U.S. or from our corporate office in Utah. The successful candidate will be responsible for leading the execution of our data governance operating model, establishing clear data management practices, and fostering a culture of data stewardship. This role requires a strategic thinker with extensive experience in data governance, capable of operating in a fast-paced environment, and collaborating effectively across various business functions.

Qualifications


5–8+ years of experience in data governance, data management, analytics, data quality, or related disciplines
Master’s degree in a relevant field
Demonstrated experience in operating and managing a data governance program
Proven ability to bring structure and clarity to ambiguous or evolving data environments
Experience working cross-functionally with business, analytics, engineering, and compliance teams
Optional: Exposure to machine learning and AI implementation
Knowledge of DevOps practices and Git version control
Track record of adopting and implementing AI-driven solutions


Responsibilities


Own the day-to-day execution of the data governance operating model across multiple data domains
Drive the creation and maintenance of governance artifacts, including business glossaries, data definitions, and critical data element (CDE) identification
Maintain metadata, data lineage, and usage context documentation to ensure data transparency and traceability
Establish, document, and communicate clear roles, responsibilities, and decision rights for data owners and stewards
Create and enforce policies and procedures to meet data governance requirements
Track and measure governance adoption and effectiveness using pragmatic metrics such as coverage, usage, and issue resolution cycle times
Serve as an escalation point for governance exceptions and data-related risk issues
Execute Requests for Proposal (RFPs) related to data governance initiatives


Benefits


Competitive compensation package with opportunities for data science and leadership growth
Comprehensive health benefits including medical, dental, vision, and life insurance
Parental leave and paid time off for personal and volunteer activities
Company-matched 401(k) retirement savings plan
Employee stock purchase program
Team-based tuition reimbursement and professional development support
Charitable gift matching to support community engagement
Provision of necessary equipment and services to support remote work
Employee Resource Group (ERG) support including Black, Women, P5ID, and ADA communities


Equal Opportunity

Progressive Leasing is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, age, veteran status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. We believe that a diverse workforce enhances our ability to innovate and serve our customers effectively.

Title: Analyst, Consumer Insights
Description:
Description

Are you mission-driven and passionate about helping others succeed?

Do you strive to lead, follow, and act with integrity?

Are you the one who honors your commitments?

Is building trusting relationships an integral part of why you do what you do?

Do you push limits and challenge the status quo?

If you answered YES, keep reading to discover how you can significantly contribute to Team USA's success!

Here's a Summary Of Responsibilities

TheFan Insights & Technologyteam sits at the center of powering the Olympic and Paralympic Movement, helping teams make data-driven decisions by bringing the voice of the fan into strategic conversations. This group delivers robust analytics across Movement organizations, teams, and commercial partners, informing critical decisions that will allow us to maximize the opportunity of two back-to-back home Games and an unprecedented decade of sport in the U.S. TheFan Insights & Technologyteam acts as a shared service supporting the U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Committee (USOPC),LA28,and our commercial joint-venture U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Properties (USOPP).

The Analyst, Consumer Insights will provide analytical, operational, and strategic support for consumer research programs across the full spectrum of needs in the Olympic and ParalympicMovement.This role will collaborate with other USOPCand LA28functional areas(including but not limited to USOPC Marketing and Strategic Impact, USOPP Partnership Marketing, Sales, and Consumer Products, LA28Marketing & Communications, and NBC Insights). TheAnalyst, Consumer Insightsshould have proven experience inquantitative and qualitativeconsumer research,social listening,synthesizing insights, and communicating findings clearly to diverse stakeholders. The ideal candidate will be a quick learner with a data-focused background and a structured thought process who is able to tackle new challenges effectively.

Here’s How You’ll Have An Impact

Consumer Research Initiatives


Support primary research initiatives fromstudydesign,project management, analysis, visualization, and presentation
Serve as the research operations and data quality lead across all primary and vendor-led quantitative research programs (e.g., survey programming, sample plans, data collection, data table creation, and organization)
Support qualitative research programs and processes (e.g., recruitment, screening, logistics, and analysis)
Manage and enhance the in-house U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Fan Community, leading the creation of new activities, recruitment, and data hygiene across member profiling
Support the fulfillment of ad-hoc fan insights requests from key stakeholders,working in close collaboration with other team members
Support research and reporting around Olympic & Paralympic Games windows


Consumer Research Program Foundation


Maintain and support the implementation of foundationalindustry best-practices, procedures, and standards across the full consumer research value chain
Ensure consistency across programs and question types as new studies are designed
Ensure that consumer research data and findings from Games windows are rigorously structured and maintained for future reference and comparability
Manage the creation and optimization of key research deliverables
Support initiatives to improve data quality, implement research best practices, andensure accuracy across deliverables


Insights Tools & Platforms


Supportprojects relying oncore syndicated data tools, configuring and querying data as needed.
Supportthe day-to-day social listening program, including underlying configuration, optimization, and reporting on ad-hoc/emerging needs, as well as collaborating on data integration with our broader infrastructure
SupportthePR/media monitoring program in collaboration with LA28 and USOPC Communications
Proactively identify opportunities to complement primary research initiatives with third-party and syndicated data sources
Perform other duties as assigned


Here are the skills and experience you'll need to be effective:


Bachelor's Degree in marketing, statistics, or related field
2-4yearsofexperienceas a data analyst,researchanalyst, or marketing analyst
Experience with primary research, survey development tools, and syndicated data
Self-motivated, independent, creative thinking, ambitious, and results-oriented, with skill in defining tasks, seeking feedback, and evolving work where applicable
Ability to work effectively in a close team environment, providing project management for various projects and initiatives
Extremely detail-oriented with excellent analytical, organizational, and time management skills
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously
Excellentwritten and oralcommunication skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Strong soft skills in client service and collaboration
Capability to distill information clearly and to explain complex issues with precision and consistency
Passion for sports and the Olympic & Paralympic Movements


Here Are The Tools, Equipment, & Conditions


Standard office equipment
Some travel domestically and internationally
Regular attendance at work is an essential job function


Here's Where You'll Work

Remote

Here's Some More Information You Need To Know


Hiring pay range $73,000 - $80,000 per year.
This Full-Time, Exempt position is intended to work 40 hours per week.
Bonus: This position will be eligible for the Annual Incentive Award Program, which has a 10% target. The USOPC may change or eliminate this program at any time at its sole discretion.
Benefits: This position will be eligible for USOPC benefits. You can view a summaryhere.


This job posting will close 12:00pm MT on 6/17/2026

Principales Habilidades

  • Análisis de inventarios
  • Organización operativa
  • Gestión de almacén
  • Manejo de Excel
  • Resolución de incidencias
  • Comunicación efectiva

Title: Analytics Engineer, SMB
Description:
About The Role

As an early member of our Data team within the Go-To-Market (GTM) organization, you will play a pivotal role in building a data-centric culture, enhancing decision-making processes, and driving strategic initiatives through analytics. This role involves a variety of projects aimed at developing canonical data sources and dashboards that enable the business to derive trustworthy, actionable insights. Most importantly, you should expect to be a core member of the GTM team, helping to bring our technology to a wide range of customers and supporting our self-serve and sales-led motions.

This role is based in either San Francisco, CA or New York, NY. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees.

In This Role, You Will


Embed with the GTM team as a trusted partner, identifying high-impact analytics problems and pioneering data-driven solutions.
Establish a data-driven culture by driving the definition, tracking, and operationalizing of metrics
Manage cross-functional data projects about revenue, pricing, usage, and other topics core to the business.
Create scalable pipelines for data collection, cleaning, and analysis from multiple sources, and manage the lifecycle of metrics and models from prototyping to production.
Develop and refine tools such as dashboards and reports that empower the team to extract and analyze data independently.
Conduct analyses to uncover insights and inform key decisions.
Use presentations, memos, and tools to communicate complex data insights clearly and persuasively across the organization.


You Might Thrive In This Role If You Are/have


Over 10 years of experience in a relevant Data role within dynamic, outcome-driven organizations.
Demonstrated ability to independently own ambiguous, high-impact business problems, from structuring the analytical approach to driving clear recommendations and execution.
Highly autonomous and resourceful, with a track record of navigating data, stakeholder, and operational blockers.
Highly skilled in SQL, with extensive experience extracting large datasets and designing ETL workflows.
Proficient in quantitative programming languages, Python preferred.
Experienced in using business intelligence tools, such as Tableau and Looker, to communicate insights and enable self-serve.
Familiar with advanced custom visualizations, such as Streamlit and Plotly Dash.
Adept at crafting clear data stories using decks, memos, and dashboards to drive decision-making at every level.
Best-in-class attention to detail and unwavering commitment to accuracy.
Proven track record of delivering significant business impact, preferably within Finance, Sales, Support, or other GTM domains.


About OpenAI

OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity.

We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement.

Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations.

To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link.

OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy

At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

Compensation Range: $220K - $335K

Title: Business Analyst, Jr. (Intelligence)
Description:
About The Company

Dane Street is a dynamic and rapidly growing organization recognized as an Inc. 500 company, renowned for its high-performance culture and innovative approach to healthcare and insurance solutions. We process over 200,000 insurance claims annually for a diverse portfolio of clients, including national and regional Workers’ Compensation, Disability, Auto, and Group Health Carriers, Third-Party Administrators, Managed Care Organizations, Employers, and Pharmacy Benefit Managers. Our core services include providing customized Independent Medical Exams and Peer Review programs that facilitate accurate medical determinations within the claims management lifecycle. At Dane Street, we prioritize excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement, fostering an environment where talented professionals can thrive and make impactful contributions.

About The Role

The Business Intelligence Analyst at Dane Street plays a pivotal role in developing and automating data reporting solutions that support both operational and strategic decision-making processes. This position is responsible for creating scalable dashboards primarily utilizing AWS QuickSight and optimizing data reporting workflows through Amazon Redshift. The analyst will work closely with cross-functional teams—including business, sales, and technical departments—to gather requirements, ensure data accuracy, and deliver actionable insights that drive business growth. Additionally, the role involves maintaining comprehensive documentation of reporting processes, supporting team training initiatives, and identifying opportunities for continuous workflow improvements. The ideal candidate will be proactive, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple projects independently while collaborating effectively within a team environment.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree preferred
3-5 years of experience in SQL for data querying and transformations
3-5 years of experience with AWS QuickSight or similar BI visualization tools
Proficiency in PC/MAC platforms, including Excel and Google Sheets
Experience in healthcare and/or insurance industries is preferred
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent oral and written communication abilities
Ability to collaborate effectively across departments
Proven ability to multitask and manage multiple priorities efficiently
Strong documentation skills for maintaining clear and comprehensive reports


Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards using AWS QuickSight to support business operations and strategic initiatives
Create scalable reporting solutions in Amazon Redshift, minimizing manual report creation and enhancing efficiency
Ensure data integrity, quality, and readiness for internal and client-facing analytics
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights that inform decision-making processes
Interpret key metrics across various departments and communicate findings effectively to stakeholders
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements, define KPIs, and deliver timely reports
Support internal and client meetings by providing clear data explanations and insights
Maintain detailed documentation for reporting workflows, templates, and processes
Identify opportunities for process improvements and implement best practices to optimize analytics workflows
Assist with ad hoc data analysis requests and support broader business initiatives as needed
Take ownership of assigned projects, demonstrating independence and proactive engagement
Support internal leadership as a subject matter expert in BI and data analytics
Adapt to changing priorities and team dynamics, contributing positively to a collaborative environment
Participate in special projects and initiatives aligned with business needs


Benefits


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and their families
Voluntary life insurance options for employees, spouses, and children
Additional voluntary benefits including hospital indemnity, critical illness, accident indemnity, and pet insurance plans
Company-provided basic life insurance, short-term disability, and long-term disability coverage at no cost
Generous paid time off policy to encourage work-life balance
401(k) retirement plan with company match to support future financial security
Provision of Apple equipment and a media stipend for remote workspace setup
Support for maintaining a healthy work environment and personal well-being


Equal Opportunity

Dane Street is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe in providing equal employment opportunities and a supportive environment where all employees can thrive and contribute to our collective success.

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and dependable Data Entry Clerk to join our team. In this role, you will play an important part in keeping our records accurate, organized, and up to date. If you enjoy working with information, have a strong eye for detail, and take pride in producing accurate work, we'd love to hear from you.

The ideal candidate is someone who values accuracy, works well independently, and understands the importance of handling sensitive information with professionalism and care.

Key Responsibilities

Enter, update, and maintain data in company databases and systems.
Review information for accuracy and completeness before inputting records.
Verify and correct data discrepancies when needed.
Organize and maintain digital and physical files.
Generate reports and retrieve information as requested by team members.
Ensure confidential information is handled securely and in accordance with company policies.
Collaborate with colleagues across departments to gather and update information.
Perform routine quality checks to maintain data integrity.
Assist with administrative tasks and other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

High school diploma or equivalent required.
Previous data entry, administrative, or clerical experience is preferred.
Strong typing skills with a high level of accuracy.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Word.
Familiarity with database systems and data management tools is an advantage.
Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.
Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.

What We're Looking For

A reliable and trustworthy individual who takes ownership of their work.
Someone who enjoys working with details and maintaining organized records.
A team player who is willing to help others and contribute to a positive work environment.
A self-motivated professional who can work independently while staying focused on quality.

Why Join Us?

We believe every role contributes to the success of our organization. As a Data Entry Clerk, your work will help ensure that important information remains accurate, accessible, and reliable for teams across the company. We offer a supportive workplace, opportunities to learn and grow, and a culture that values respect, collaboration, and attention to detail.

Title: Data Analyst - Business Intelligence
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU’LL DO​



As a Business Intelligence Analyst within Central Business Analytics at H&M Group, you will play a key role in transforming data into accessible, impactful insights for decision-makers across the organization. Working close to the core business, you will help shape and scale H&M’s analytical toolkit by building intuitive, reliable, and business‑relevant BI solutions that empower thousands of users globally.



In this role, you combine strong technical BI skills with a genuine curiosity for the business. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders across functions to understand their analytical needs and translate them into clear, actionable reporting and datasets that support better, faster decisions.



To succeed in this role, you will…​



Build, maintain, and continuously improve reports, data models, and BI solutions using the Microsoft Power BI platform.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand their questions, challenges, and priorities, ensuring insights delivered have real business impact.
Transform large and complex datasets into clear, user‑friendly visualizations and data stories.
Collaborate with IT and data platform teams to ensure the data environment supports scalable and reliable BI solutions.
Contribute to best practices, knowledge sharing, and capability building within the BI and data community.





WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH​



Business Analytics is a central unit working closely with Group Controlling, Strategy, and Business Development functions. Our mission is to deliver relevant and reliable decision support that accelerates H&M’s strategic plans and improves our decision making. Our function plays a key role in enabling fast, data-driven, and trustworthy insights that support H&M Group’s top priorities.







WHO YOU ARE​



We are looking for people with…​



A degree in a quantitative field such as engineering, business, economics, or statistics.
Experience in data, insights, or BI roles, in-house or as a consultant.
A genuine interest in creating business value through data and analytics.
Experience working with large datasets and complex analytical problems.
Strong skills in Power BI (or similar tools), including data modelling and visualization.
Good knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
A passion for data visualization and clear data storytelling.



And people who are…​



Self‑driven, structured, and pragmatic in their way of working.
Communicative team players who enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders.
Curious and eager to learn, actively following developments in BI and analytics.
Motivated by making insights accessible and useful for others.
Aligned with H&M’s values, with a passion for growth, innovation, and creating customer value.​

WHO WE ARE​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​



​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​



​All our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP)
All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET.
A dynamic, entrepreneurial environment where your insights directly influence strategic decisions.
A collaborative culture that values innovation, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.





JOIN US​



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.



The role is based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.



Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!​





*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

  • Experience in data, insights, or BI roles, in-house or as a consultant.
  • A genuine interest in creating business value through data and analytics.
  • Experience working with large datasets and complex analytical problems.
  • Strong skills in Power BI (or similar tools), including data modelling and visualization.
  • Good knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
  • A passion for data visualization and clear data storytelling.



And people who are…​

  • Self‑driven, structured, and pragmatic in their way of working.
  • Communicative team players who enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders.
  • Curious and eager to learn, actively following developments in BI and analytics.
  • Motivated by making insights accessible and useful for others.
  • Aligned with H&M’s values, with a passion for growth, innovation, and creating customer value.​

WHO WE ARE​

H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​

​

WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​

  • ​All our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP)
  • All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET.
  • A dynamic, entrepreneurial environment where your insights directly influence strategic decisions.
  • A collaborative culture that values innovation, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.



JOIN US​

Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.

The role is based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.

Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!​



*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

Title: Data Analyst (H/F)
Description:
Notre client, acteur du secteur financier et basé à Paris est à la recherche de leur Data Analyst pour piloter le développement de notre environnement analytique et fournir des analyses basées sur les données à notre équipe dirigeante et à nos actionnaires.

Rattaché(e) au Responsable FP&A, le/la Data Analyst a pour mission de centraliser, structurer et fiabiliser l’ensemble des données afin de produire des analyses pertinentes et des reportings stratégiques à destination de la Direction. Il/elle contribue activement à la transformation data de l’entreprise et à l’amélioration du pilotage de la performance.





Responsabilités clé

sCentralisation et structuration des donnée

sRecenser les différentes sources de données internes : CRM, Outils métiers, données assureurs
;Centraliser les jeux de données principaux dans notre entrepôt de données (DWH) pour des workflows efficaces et automatisés
;Nettoyer, structurer et contrôler la qualité des données
;Créer des rapports BI clairs et pertinents, rendant les messages clés aussi accessibles que possible pour les parties prenantes. Garantir l’exactitude, la cohérence et la performance des données dans les tableaux de bord et rapports. Documenter les analyses et les méthodologies utilisées, appliquer les règles de gouvernance et de sécurité des données et participer au respect des exigences réglementaires (ex. : traçabilité, auditabilité)
.Automatiser les reportings mensuels et/ou trimestriels




.

onIdentifier des axes d’améliorations afin d’orienter les décisions stratégiques
;Participer aux travaux budgétaires et aux prévisions avec la Direction FP&A
;Automatiser les reportings mensuels et/ou trimestriels
;Utiliser ces livrables pour fournir à l’équipe dirigeante des recommandations stratégiques sur des sujets clés tels que la fidélisation des clients, le taux d’équipement des clients, la productivité commerciale et opérationnell




e.

é :Vous êtes issu(e) d’une formation Bac+5 en Informatique, statistiques, data, mathématiques ou informatique et disposez d’une première expérience en analyse de données, business intelligence et finance, idéalement acquise dans un environnement en forte croissan

ce.Vous manifestez un intérêt marqué pour les environnements financiers et vous appuyez sur de solides compétences techniques : excellente maîtrise de SQL pour l’extraction et la structuration des données, de Python pour l’automatisation et l’analyse avancée, ainsi que de Power BI pour la création de tableaux de bord et la visualisation des indicateurs de performan

ce.À l’aise avec Excel et dans un environnement Microsoft, vous êtes en mesure de transformer des données complexes en analyses claires et pertinentes, facilitant la prise de décision auprès des parties prenantes de haut niveau, notamment le CFO et les membres du comité exécut

Title: Data Analyst Italy
Description:
Reports to: Digital CRM & Media Director Italy

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: Milan, Italy

About The Role

As digitalization accelerates across all divisions, our business model increasingly relies on seamless digital and physical client journeys. To support this transformation, we are seeking a Data Analyst to join our Digital Team in Italy.

Your mission: leverage data for actionable client insights that empower all our divisions and markets.

Key Responsibilities



Collect and analyze data from multiple sources
Create clear dashboards and reports to present analytical findings
Interpret results to identify key trends, opportunities, and challenges
Collaborate closely with Divisions to understand data needs and develop tailored analyses and solutions
Maintain and enhance data systems and analytical tools to ensure accuracy and relevance
Conduct complex analyses and synthesize findings into impactful, decision-ready reporting



Required Qualifications



Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, Economics, Data Analytics, Marketing, or related field
2–4 years of experience in a data/analytics role, ideally in retail, luxury, or CRM
Strong proficiency in Excel and Power BI
Familiarity with Salesforce and Imagino
Fluent in English; Italian is a strong plus



Desired Skills & Attributes



Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for diverse stakeholders
Strong communication skills and ability to train non-technical users
Comfortable managing multiple deliverables across three Divisions simultaneously
Proactive, autonomous, and passionate about the luxury industry

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
📊 Data Analyst | Data & AI

📍 Toulouse, France




Au sein d'une direction Data & Intelligence Artificielle d'un groupe industriel international de haute technologie, contribution à la valorisation des données et au développement de solutions analytiques permettant d'accompagner les métiers dans leurs décisions et leur transformation

digitale.




🚀 Principales responsabilités

Collecter, préparer et analyser des données issues de multiples sources afin de produire des indicateurs fiables et exploitables.
Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord et reportings à destination des équipes opérationnelles et du management.
Participer à la mise en œuvre et à l'industrialisation des flux de données en collaboration avec les équipes Data Engineering.
Développer et maintenir des modèles de transformation de données avec dbt afin de garantir la qualité, la traçabilité et la gouvernance des données.
Exploiter les services Google Cloud Platform (GCP) pour le stockage, la transformation et l'analyse des données à grande échelle.
Réaliser des analyses exploratoires et identifier des leviers d'amélioration des processus métiers.
Automatiser les processus de reporting et optimiser les chaînes de traitement des données.
Collaborer avec les équipes Data, IA et les différentes parties prenantes dans un environnement Agile.
Participer à la diffusion des bonnes pratiques et à la promotion d'une culture Data au sein de l'organisation.




🛠️ Environnement technique

Google Cloud Platform (GCP) • BigQuery • dbt • Python • SQL • Power BI • Pandas • Git • Jupyter Notebook • ETL/ELT • Data Visualization • Machine Learning (fondamentaux) • Méthodologies Agile




🎯 Compétences clés

Analyse et modélisation de données
Développement de pipelines ELT
Data Quality & Data Governance
Business Intelligence et visualisation
Reporting et automatisation
Collaboration transverse avec les équipes Data Engineering, IA et métiers
Vulgarisation et restitution des résultats
Amélioration continue et méthodes Agile

Title: Reporting Data Analyst
Description:
We offer:

A supportive and vibrant multinational environment, we accept individual differences and believe in collaborative team working
Flexible working arrangements (core hours and opportunity to work from home)
Inclusive and welcoming environment
Contingent/Leased assignment working onsite at Morgan Stanley
Contract via 3rd party agency




You will:

Support credit risk reporting activities, including recurring reporting processes and committee deliverables
Liaise with reporting colleagues, risk managers and partner teams to deliver reporting outputs and datasets
Support the creation and maintenance of Credit Risk datasets, data rules and workflows in our ETL tool
Analyze existing reports and SQL logic to understand and document dataset requirements
Work with technology and data teams on data onboarding, testing and rollout of datasets
Support UAT activities, including test case preparation, execution and issue tracking
Work with data consumers to provide the required datasets for reporting and analytics
Maintain Credit Risk datasets in dataset assembly tools and support curation activities
Escalate data issues and support resolution in collaboration with Data Control and technology teams
Document reporting processes, business rules




You have:

Bachelor’s or higher degree
2–4 years' experience in a similar field
Strong Excel skills (including VBA, Power Query)
Strong analytical mindset
SQL / database experience / data analysis
Good communication and problem-solving skills in English
Strong attention to detail and ability to work in a fast-paced environment




Nice to have:

Previous experience in banking, financial services, risk management or regulatory reporting
PowerBi / Python and Data management experience is a plus
Interest in process automation and continuous improvement
Experience preparing materials for senior stakeholders




Temp profile:

The CRM Reporting team is responsible for developing and maintaining the credit risk datasets and reporting framework. The team collaborates with partners across the globe from many different business and technology groups. We are creating curated data marts: multi-purpose data sources for various teams to use. We are also responsible for senior management reporting, producing committee materials, maintaining automations and working with risk managers on the content of the reports. We are seeking a temporary candidate to support both data curation and reporting activities, contributing to the production of committee materials and enabling consistent, high-quality credit risk reporting.

About us:

Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing a wide range of investment banking, securities, investment management and wealth management services. As a market leader, the talent and passion of our people is critical to our success. Together, we share a common set of values rooted in integrity, excellence, and strong team ethics. We can provide a superior foundation for building a professional career – a place for people to learn, to achieve and grow. A philosophy that balances personal lifestyles, perspectives and needs is an important part of our culture.

Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunities employer. We work to provide a supportive and inclusive environment where all individuals can maximize their full potential. Our skilled and creative workforce is comprised of individuals drawn from a broad cross section of the global communities in which we operate and who reflect a variety of backgrounds, talents, perspectives, and experiences. Our strong commitment to a culture of inclusion is evident through our constant focus on recruiting, developing, and advancing individuals based on their skills and talents.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Greetings from Ubique Systems!!!







We are looking for a Data Analyst for one of our customer who has an expertise in:-







Required Skills & Experience

Strong experience in Data Engineering, data modeling, data analysis, and enterprise data architecture
Expertise in relational and dimensional data modeling (Star/Snowflake schemas)
Deep understanding of data warehousing, data lakes, and lakehouse concepts
Hands-on experience with databases such as SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and Redshift
Strong SQL programming, query optimization, and analytical skills
Hands-on experience in ETL/ELT development using DBT and tools such as Informatica, Talend, Azure Data Factory, AWS Glue, or similar
Experience designing and supporting distributed data systems and scalable cloud-based data platforms
Strong exposure to CI/CD pipelines and version control tools such as Git, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps
Hands-on experience with AWS data services including AWS Glue, S3, Redshift, Lambda, Athena, and EMR
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Proficiency in data modeling and metadata management tools such as ERwin, ER/Studio, PowerDesigner, or similar
Familiarity with data governance, catalog, and lineage tools such as Collibra, Alation, Apache Atlas, or Informatica EDC
Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments
Experience working closely with Data Engineering teams on enterprise-scale data transformation and modernization programs




Good to Have / Preferred Technical Skills

Hands-on experience with big data technologies such as Spark, Databricks, Hadoop, or Kafka
Experience with Python or PySpark for data analysis, transformation, and automation
Exposure to BI and reporting tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or QuickSight
Knowledge of APIs, microservices-based data integration, and real-time/streaming data pipelines
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation) and container technologies such as Docker or Kubernetes
Understanding of Data Vault 2.0 modeling concepts
Exposure to AI/ML data preparation pipelines and feature engineering concepts
Experience in Pharma, Healthcare, or Life Sciences domains will carry strong preference

R&D or innovation-focused project experience is good to have










Employment type:-Contract.







It would be Hybrid working within Warsaw, Mazowieckie, Poland only.







Note:-Only apply if you have valid visa in the EU Region, Sponsorship will not be given.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Business Data Analyst!




Responsabilities:

Gather and analyze requirements from business stakeholders, data owners, and regulatory teams.
Translate business needs into functional specifications (user stories, acceptance criteria, data‑model diagrams).
Create and maintain documentation (requirements catalog, data dictionary, process maps, test plans).
Facilitate test planning by participating in design test cases
Communicate progress and outcomes to stakeholders through sprint demos, status reports and reviews.
Identify improvement opportunities in the transversal framework, propose enhancements.
Collaborate with the ETL development team to design optimal data‑flow and transformation logic.
Define, implement, and maintain the data‑quality framework (rules, thresholds, validation checks).




Requirements:

SQL development skills (Oracle is the DB used).
PL/SQL development skills.
IBM Datastage PX and/or other similar ETL tools.
Agile Project‑Management Tools – Manage backlogs, user stories, and traceability in Jira or Azure DevOps; ensure requirements are linked to test cases and delivery artifacts.
Data Analysis & Visualization – Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA), statistical summarization, and create visual insights (Power BI, Tableau, or Excel) to support requirement‐gathering and decision‑making.
Data‑Quality & Profiling Techniques – Use data‑profiling methods to define validation rules, cleansing procedures, and quality‑metric dashboards.
Good level of english.




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context.
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally.
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust.
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/BusinessDataAnalyst".




Your next challenge might start here.

Join us!😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst (H/F)
Description:
Leader de l’Ingénierie et des IT Services, présent dans plus de 30 pays, le groupe ALTEN accompagne la stratégie de développement de ses clients dans les domaines de l’innovation, de la R&D et des systèmes d'information technologiques.

Plus qu’un poste, découvrez l’aventure qui vous attend au sein d’une entreprise engagée.





Description du poste



Votre environnement



Vous intégrez une équipe Data intervenant sur des projets de valorisation et d’analyse de données au service de programmes aéronautiques et défense, dans un environnement industriel à forte exigence de fiabilité et de qualité des données.



Les projets visent à améliorer la prise de décision, le pilotage des activités et la performance opérationnelle, à partir de données issues de systèmes complexes.



Les missions s’inscrivent dans un contexte long terme, avec des projets structurants et des perspectives d’évolution.



Vos missions



En tant que Data Analyst, vous contribuez à la transformation des données en informations exploitables pour les équipes métier :



Analyser et exploiter des jeux de données issus de différentes sources.



Concevoir et maintenir des tableaux de bord et rapports à destination des équipes métier.



Participer à la définition des indicateurs de performance (KPI).



Assurer la qualité, la cohérence et la fiabilité des données.



Collaborer avec les équipes métier pour comprendre les besoins d’analyse et de reporting.



Contribuer à l’amélioration des processus de collecte et de traitement des données.



Documenter les analyses et les modèles de données.





Qualifications



Profil recherché



Issu(e) d’une formation en informatique, data ou équivalent, vous justifiez d’une première expérience en analyse de données.



Vous êtes à l’aise pour manipuler les données, les restituer de manière claire et accompagner les utilisateurs dans leur compréhension.



Compétences appréciées



Maîtrise des outils de data analysis (SQL, Excel avancé)



Expérience avec des outils de data visualisation (Power BI, Tableau ou équivalent)



Connaissance des bases de données relationnelles



Sens de l’analyse, rigueur et esprit de synthèse



Bon relationnel et capacité à travailler avec des équipes métier





Informations supplémentaires



🎯 Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?



Des projets Data à forte valeur ajoutée dans les secteurs aéronautique et défense



Des missions long terme offrant visibilité et stabilité



Une montée en compétences sur des technologies et outils Data modernes (SQL, ETL, BI, Cloud, IA)



L’opportunité de contribuer à l’industrialisation des données et à la valorisation de l’information



Un accompagnement personnalisé tout au long de la mission par les équipes ALTEN

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Partner in Pet Food (PPF) is an innovative, leading European pet food producer, supplying over 400 customers, including major grocery retailers, supermarkets, specialized pet shops in the 35 markets in which it operates.

PPF is the second largest producer of Retailer Brand wet and dry pet food in Europe, offering a full range of high quality cat and dog food products.

PPF employs around 3,000 staff and has operations in 10 countries.




We are looking for a Data Analyst, who would like to be a part of our international team.




Duties and responsibilities:




Data Discovery & Requirements Gathering

Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather reporting and data requirements.
Identify key data sources and define KPIs, metrics, and reporting hierarchies.
Analyze existing data structures and business processes to inform ERP design.

Data Migration & Mapping

Map legacy data to the new ERP structure.
Cleanse and standardize master data (e.g., customers, vendors, materials, GL accounts).
Support data enrichment and validation activities.
Validate data transformation logic and perform reconciliation post-migration.
Ensure data integrity and consistency across systems.

Reporting & Analytics

Design and prototype dashboards and reports using BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Infor EPM).
Translate business requirements into data models and queries.
Ensure master data supports accurate and meaningful reporting.

Master Data Governance

Define and document data standards, naming conventions, and validation rules.
Support implementation of master data governance frameworks and tools.
Collaborate with data stewards and business data owners to maintain data quality.

Testing & Validation

Conduct testing for reports and dashboards.
Validate data accuracy and completeness in ERP and reporting outputs.

Documentation & Training

Document data definitions, business rules, and reporting logic.
Provide training and support to end-users on data usage and interpretation.

Post-Go-Live Support

Monitor data quality and reporting performance.
Identify and resolve data-related issues.
Recommend continuous improvement initiatives for data management.




You are the right fit, if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Information Systems, Business, or related field.
3+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in ERP implementation projects.
Strong understanding of master data domains and data governance principles.
Proficiency in SQL and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI).
Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, Infor) is a plus.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Knowledge of data migration tools and ETL processes.
Familiarity with data quality tools and frameworks.
Knowledge of financial and supply chain data structures.
Experience in FMCG industries.




What we offer:

Excellent salary
SZÉP Card
Home office opportunity
Commuting allowance
Free Fitness opportunity
Pet-friendly office
Health Insurance
Free parking opportunities
Free language classes (English/Hun)
Online Employee Shop
Unlimited mobile phone use
Wellbeing programs and seminars

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Would you like to work with the latest technology in the automotive industry?

Bertrandt AG Spain is an innovative German multinational company that provides engineering solutions in the automotive sector. We are more than 500 employees, and our goal is to keep growing.

Teamwork is the fuel that drives us to excellence, working hand in hand with our clients. We are people who share the same passion — would you like to join our team?




About the Role

We are looking for a skilled and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will play a key part in ensuring data reliability, quality, and usability across the organization. You will work with multiple data sources and collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement.




Key Responsibilities

Analyze, validate, and monitor data quality across various systems and datasets
Process and manipulate data using Python and SQL
Develop and maintain dashboards and visualizations using Looker Studio and Microsoft Power BI
Identify data inconsistencies, trends, and opportunities for optimization
Produce insightful reports to support business and operational decisions
Document data sources, methodologies, and transformation processes
Contribute to the continuous improvement of data quality and data management practices




What You’ll Deliver

High-quality, validated datasets
Analytical reports highlighting key findings and improvement opportunities
Interactive dashboards and visualizations
Actionable insights for stakeholders
Clear and structured documentation of data processes
Recommendations to enhance data quality and efficiency




Data You Will Work With

Data from internal systems (Engineering, Operations, and other departments)
Master data and reference datasets
Operational and production data
Historical and real-time data (where applicable)
Existing reports and data models




Required Skills and Experience

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing
Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Looker Studio)
Solid understanding of data quality and master data management principles
Ability to work with large and complex datasets
Experience identifying trends, anomalies, and optimization opportunities
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams
Structured, critical-thinking mindset with a focus on continuous improvement




Tools & Environment

You will have access to internal data systems, business intelligence tools, data extraction platforms, and collaboration tools necessary to perform your role effectively.




What can we offer you?

🌏 Permanent contract

🕓 Flexible working hours

📚 Language training programs and continuous learning opportunities

🚑 Flexible compensation plan (including health insurance…)

🎉 Company events — we like to have fun!

💖 A great working environment, open and inclusive




If you think this role is for you and you would like to work in a dynamic and continuously learning environment, apply now — we look forward to meeting you!

We are BERTRANDT, and you?

We look forward to meeting you!

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

The Senior Business Analyst at client will be responsible for data analysis, business analysis, and Agile methodology to support various projects and initiatives. The role involves interpreting and analyzing complex data sets, identifying trends, and providing valuable insights to drive business decisions. Additionally, the Senior Business Analyst will collaborate with cross functional teams to gather requirements, prioritize tasks, and ensure successful project delivery.

Key Responsibilities

1. Conduct thorough data analysis to support strategic decision-making processes

2. Work closely with stakeholders to gather and document business requirements

3. Utilize agile methodology to facilitate project planning, execution, and delivery

4. Identify opportunities for process improvement and efficiency enhancements

5. Prepare and present reports and recommendations based on data analysis findings

6. Collaborate with it teams to ensure alignment between business objectives and technical solutions

7. Participate in user acceptance testing (uat) and provide support during implementation phase

8. Stay updated on industry best practices in data analysis, business analysis, and agile methodologies




Skill Requirements

1. Proficiency in data analysis techniques and tools

2. Strong business acumen and understanding of business processes

3. Experience in agile methodology, including scrum and kanban frameworks

4. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

5. Ability to work effectively in a team environment and independently

6. Critical thinking and problem-solving abilities

7. Detail oriented with a focus on accuracy and quality

8. Strong organizational and time management skills




Certifications: Agile Certified Practitioner (ACP) or Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP) certification preferred

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Labor-b è una realtà in crescita che ha come sua mission quella di aiutare le aziende, i loro imprenditori e imprenditrici, a realizzare il proprio modello strategico di cultura d'Impresa. Crediamo in progetti esclusivi nel settore delle Risorse Umane costruiti partendo dai punti di forza e dai valori in cui l'impresa si riconosce. 

Per la nostra sede di Chiusi stiamo cercando un/una Junior Data Analyst in stage che possa supportare il team nella trasformazione di dati grezzi in insight significativi per guidare la strategia aziendale. L'obiettivo principale è raccogliere, pulire e interpretare i dati per identificare tendenze, metriche chiave e informazioni utili a migliorare le performance aziendali e supportare i processi decisionali.

Di cosa ti occuperai?

Raccolta e Pulizia Dati: Acquisire dati da diverse fonti (es. database aziendali, fogli di calcolo, API) e prepararli per l'analisi (rimozione duplicati, gestione dati mancanti, standardizzazione)
Elaborazione Analitica: Eseguire analisi statistiche, esplorative e predittive per scoprire pattern e correlazioni utilizzando tool specifici e, se richiesto, linguaggi di programmazione come Python o R
Visualizzazione: Creare visualizzazioni chiare ed efficaci (es. grafici, tabelle pivot) e sviluppare dashboard dinamiche con strumenti di Business Intelligence (es. Power BI, Tableau)
Comunicazione: Preparare presentazioni e report riassuntivi per comunicare i risultati delle analisi al management e ai team, traducendo dati complessi in informazioni semplici e actionable
Collaborazione: Lavorare a stretto contatto con i colleghi più senior e i vari dipartimenti per definire i requisiti di analisi e supportare l'implementazione di soluzioni analitiche
Documentazione: Mantenere una documentazione accurata di tutte le analisi e dei modelli creati, garantendo la replicabilità e la trasparenza del lavoro svolto
Monitoraggio delle Performance: Contribuire al monitoraggio delle performance aziendali, supportando l'analisi degli scostamenti rispetto agli obiettivi e ai budget stabiliti


Requirements



Il tuo identikit


Diploma o Laurea in discipline scientifiche (es. Informatica, Ingegneria Informatica, Statistica) o economiche/gestionali
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (scritta e parlata)
Ottima conoscenza di almeno uno strumento di Business Intelligence (es. Microsoft Power BI, Tableau).
Conoscenza basica di sistemi di gestione presenze ( Zucchetti, Teamsystem, etc)
Buona conoscenza di Python o R per l'analisi e la manipolazione dei dati
Competenza nell'uso di SQL per l'interrogazione e la gestione di database
Ottima padronanza di Microsoft Excel (funzioni avanzate, tabelle pivot)
Capacità di problem solving
Metodicità e precisione nella gestione dei dati e nell'analisi
Abilità nel presentare risultati tecnici a un pubblico non tecnico in modo chiaro e conciso
Flessibilità e proattività nell'acquisire nuove competenze e rimanere aggiornati sugli sviluppi del settore


Benefits



Cosa ti offriamo?

Entrare a far parte di Labor-b significa:

Far parte di W Group, una delle più grandi piattaforme HR internazionali
Inserimento in stage di 6 mesi, finalizzato all'assunzione
Settimana corta: 4 gg lavorativi anziché 5 a parità di stipendio e condizioni normative
Buoni pasto da 8€


Il valore di far parte di Labor-b

Il nostro impegno è valorizzare il tuo lavoro; la nostra priorità è quella di sviluppare le capacità delle persone che entrano a far parte del nostro Team!

Cerchiamo agenti di cambiamento. Vogliamo persone che si sentono a loro agio quando vengono richiesti loro dei risultati su obiettivi specifici, poiché solo i risultati portano vero cambiamento.

Diventa un #agentedicambiamento. Entra nel team di Labor-b!

Title: Alternance - Data Analyst
Description:
Ready to take on meaningful challenges and shape the next generation of mobility?

Joining OPmobility means stepping into a global group with a purpose to keep people and goods moving safely, smarter, and sustainably everywhere.

Objectif du poste

En tant qu’alternant Data Analyst spécialisé en sustainability au sein de l’équipe business, vous contribuez à la structuration, à l’analyse et à la valorisation des données. Vous participez à la création de jeux de données et des dashboards permettant de piloter les activités liées au développement durable : Bilan Carbone, CSRD, etc

Missions principales

Recueillir et analyser les besoins des différentes parties prenantes relatifs aux KPIs sustainability.

Collecter, fiabiliser et consolider les données au sein du Data Lake afin de garantir leur qualité, leur cohérence et leur disponibilité pour les analyses.

Concevoir et développer des maquettes et des Proofs of Concept (POCs) de dashboards sous Power BI.

Collaborer avec les Data Managers et contribuer activement à la communauté Data.

Compétences requises

Bonne maîtrise de SQL et Power BI.

Solides capacités d’analyse et esprit critique.

Aisance relationnelle et capacité à vulgariser des concepts techniques.

Curiosité et fort intérêt pour la data.

Maîtrise de l’anglais.

At OPmobility, people truly matter. We are committed to building inclusive teams, promoting diversity and equality, and ensuring that every application is considered fairly - because the future of mobility is built by diverse perspectives, bold ideas, and people who dare to move forward.

Innovation is therefore not a buzzword, but a natural part of everyday work. You’ll grow in an international environment where cutting-edge technologies, industrial excellence, and real-world impact come together to tackle the challenges of tomorrow’s automotive industry.

Founded in 1946 by Pierre Burelle, OPmobility, known until 2024 as Plastic Omnium, has transformed itself into a player in sustainable and connected mobility. Today, OPmobility develops technological solutions across four areas of expertise: exterior and lighting systems, the integration of complex modules, technologies related to energy storage, hydrogen and electrification, and a division dedicated to the development of embedded software and digital solutions.

With €11.5 billion in revenue in 2025, 152 factories, 40 R&D centers, and nearly 38,100 employees across 28 countries, OPmobility combines global scale with local impact. All driven by a shared ambition to accelerate the automotive energy transition.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Urgent Hire: Data Analyst


•          Start date: ASAP
•          Duration: 12 months
•          Location: 1 week on site, Dublin - 3 weeks working from home
•          Rate: €200- €250 per day


Description:
 
Bachelor’s Degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience
Experience working as a Data Analyst (typically 1–2 years), ideally within financial services or a data-rich environment; we will also consider recent graduates with relevant skills or internships
Experience with reference data, data management, and data analytics
Working knowledge of tools and techniques for analysing and presenting data, such as SQL, Python/R, and Power BI (or similar tools)
Familiarity with data vendor products (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters) is beneficial
Interest in or exposure to advanced analytics, anomaly detection, or AI-driven data solutions is an advantage

The Purpose of Your Role

In this role, you will balance day-to-day data quality and production activities with analytical and project-based work, with a strong focus on quality and continuous improvement.

You will also have the opportunity to contribute to the evolution of our data capabilities, including the use of automation, anomaly detection, and AI techniques to enhance data quality, efficiency, and insight generation.


The Skills You Bring
 

A proactive and curious mindset, with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively
Strong investigative and analytical skills, with the ability to quickly understand complex issues and identify patterns or anomalies in data
An interest in innovation and continuous process improvement
Clear written and verbal communication skills, particularly when explaining complex topics
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build effective relationships with business partners
The ability to manage priorities and deliver against both short-term deadlines and longer-term objectives

The Value You Deliver
 

Deliver high-quality, accurate, and timely data analysis and insights
Identify and implement process improvements to enhance efficiency and data quality
Provide root cause analysis and insights into data challenges, including identifying anomalies and trends
Collaborate with business and technology partners to resolve issues and improve processes
Support the adoption of automation and AI-driven approaches to strengthen data management
Proactively contribute to and support a range of projects and initiatives

Title: Junior / Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
At a glance 📌

Position: Junior Data Analyst

Location: Madrid

Experience: Recent graduates or candidates with up to 1 year of experience




Want to know more? 👇

Chemo is a global pharmaceutical company specialized in the development, manufacturing, and commercialization of active pharmaceutical ingredients, finished dosage forms, and generics. As part of the Insud Pharma group, Chemo plays a key role across the pharmaceutical value chain, with a strong focus on quality, innovation, and sustainable growth.

If you have recently completed — or are about to complete — a Master’s in Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Business Analytics, Data Science, or a related field, and you are looking to start your career in a role that combines data, business, and strategy, this opportunity could be for you 💪

At Chemo, we are looking for a Junior Data Analyst to support the Business Development & Portfolio team, helping analyze data and strategic information to identify, assess, and prioritize new product opportunities.

This role is designed for someone with strong potential, analytical curiosity, and a willingness to learn, who wants to grow in an environment where data has a real impact on decision-making.




What are we looking for? 🔎

We are looking for someone with an analytical mindset, an interest in working with data, and a business-oriented approach, who is eager to learn how to transform complex information into useful insights that support strategic decisions.

We are not necessarily looking for extensive professional experience, but rather for a solid foundation, a learning mindset, and motivation to grow in an international pharma environment.




The challenge 🚀

Support the collection, organization, and analysis of market, competitor, and internal data.
Assist in the preparation and maintenance of reports, metrics, benchmarks, and dashboards.
Help structure, clean, and transform data for analysis.
Contribute to quantitative analyses aimed at identifying product opportunities and market trends.
Support the generation of insights for the Portfolio and Business Development teams.
Work closely with both technical and business stakeholders to understand needs and translate them into useful analyses.
Learn and progressively develop automation and data analysis capabilities within the team.




What do you need? 🤓

A recently completed — or nearly completed — Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Business Analytics, Data Science, or a related field.
Foundational knowledge of tools such as Python, SQL, and Excel.
Interest in applying data analysis to business decisions.
Strong analytical thinking, curiosity, and eagerness to learn.
Good organizational skills and attention to detail.
Ability to communicate ideas clearly and work with non-technical stakeholders.
A level of English that allows you to work in an international environment and handle documentation/information comfortably.

It would be great if you also have ✅

Knowledge of visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Experience with academic or personal projects related to data analysis.
Interest in the pharma, healthcare, or regulated industries.
Basic understanding of automation, ETL, or data preparation processes.
A genuine interest in combining data + business + strategy.




What do we offer? 👏

📍 Hybrid position in Madrid (4/1)

🎓 A strong learning and development environment for junior profiles

🤝 Close support and mentoring from the team

📈 The opportunity to grow in a role with high business exposure

🚀 Involvement in analyses that have a real impact on strategic decisions

🌍 An international, dynamic, and innovative environment within the pharmaceutical industry




Why could this be a great opportunity for you?

Because this is not a purely technical role or a purely business role — it is an opportunity to develop a broader profile, learning how to work with data in a real business context and how to turn information into actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.
It is a great first step if you want to grow at the intersection of analytics, business, and pharma.




What does the selection process look like? 🕵️

Initial contact by phone or email.
Interview with Talent Acquisition.
Interview with the Hiring Manager / team.
Communication and follow-up throughout the process.




🔷 Think this role may not be for you? 🔷

Follow us on LinkedIn and Instagram to stay updated on new opportunities.

#Chemo #InsudPharma #Challenger #Insuder #InsudTalent







EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES COMMITMENT

At Chemo, we are committed to equal opportunities and non-discrimination. We welcome all applications regardless of gender, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, or any other personal or social condition protected by applicable law.

HTML:
About the Role:

For a client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a interim Data Analyst. The ideal candidate will use their passion for big data and analytics to provide insights to the business covering a range of topics. They will be responsible for conducting both recurring and ad hoc analysis for business users.


Responsibilities

  • Understand the day-to-day issues that our business faces, which can be better understood with data;
  • Compile and analyse data related to business' issues;
  • Develop clear visualisations to convey complicated data in a straightforward manner

  • 1-3 years of experience;
  • Hands-on experience with SQL;
  • Working experience with Python and/or PySpark;
  • Working experience with Tableau or PowerBI;
  • Stakeholder management and communication skills;
  • Must be based in the Netherlands (sponsorship not offered)


What We Offer:

  • An interim position
  • Flexible Working: Hybrid model, with 2–3 days in the office in Amsterdam (non-negotiable)

Title: ALTERNANCE - Assistant Data Analyst F/H
Description:
Poste

From the house of dream to the house of talents

« Quoi que vous fassiez - pour le travail ou le plaisir - faites-le avec passion ! Vivez avec passion... »* Christian Dior

Christian Dior fut le couturier du rêve. Dès la fondation de sa Maison en 1947, marquée par la révolution du New Look, il métamorphose ses songes en de merveilleuses créations. Son esprit visionnaire n’a eu de cesse de magnifier les femmes du monde entier, inspirant aujourd’hui et plus que jamais ses successeurs. « Le tissu est le seul véhicule de nos rêves (…). La mode, en somme, est issue d’un rêve, et le rêve, c’est une évasion », écrivait-il dans son ouvrage Je suis couturier. Au fil de ses collections, Monsieur Dior est devenu le démiurge du bonheur de ses muses.

Aujourd’hui, riche de cet héritage d’exception et d’une communauté de plus de 7 500 talents, notre Maison incarne plus que jamais la Maison des Talents pensé par Christian Dior. Dans plus de 40 pays, nous faisons rayonner notre savoir-faire, notre audace et notre excellence à travers la passion et la créativité de nos collaborateurs. Rejoignez-nous et créez votre avenir dans notre univers généreux, disruptif et ambitieux où nous grandissons dans l’excellence, le courage et l’optimisme.


Le Petit Dictionnaire de la Mode


Missions

DETAIL DE LA MISSION

Contexte

Au sein de l’Analytics Factory (siège Monde), vous assisterez l’équipe dans la production et l’analyse des données clients afin d’aider aux prises de décisions. Vous serez amené à être en contact avec nos interlocuteurs en Zone ainsi que les divisions Produits et équipes Marketing Client, dans le but de les soutenir dans leur suivi client.

Descriptif

En fonction de votre profil, nous pourrons définir une mission principale : réalisation d’un score d’appétence produits, retravailler la segmentation clients actuelle, affiner la définition des clients inactifs pour mettre en place des traitements différenciés, …

En Périphérie De Cette Mission Principale, Vous Participerez à La Production Et à L’analyse Quotidiennes Des Données Clients


Réalisation d’études et d’analyses clients (analyse du comportement d’achat, profils clients, parcours clients, segmentations)
Analyses des performances des produits et des collections (ventes, tendances, recrutement etc.)
Gestion opérationnelle des campagnes marketing : recommandation de ciblage, comptages et créations
Mise en place de tableaux de bord afin de suivre la performance des campagnes (newsletters, mailing…)
Analyse des impacts des différentes actions clientèles monde (événements clients, …)


Profil

De formation Bac+5, type école d’ingénieur ou université spécialisée en statistiques


Dataiku / SQL indispensable
Rigueur, fiabilité et autonomie
Esprit d’équipe
Anglais indispensable


Ce que vous apprendrez au cours de cette alternance :

Vous pourrez développer vos compétences analytiques (lecture de résultats, analyse et communication pertinente des enseignements).

Vous aurez également l’occasion de mettre en application vos compétences statistiques à l’aide notamment de Dataiku, dont vous pourrez de plus améliorer votre maîtrise.

Vous participerez à la vie courante d’un grand groupe de luxe, en découvrirez l’univers et les problématiques clients.

A titre personnel, vous développerez vos compétences relationnelles au contact des différentes équipes en Zone, Marketing Client et des divisions Produit, ainsi que votre rigueur et votre autonomie.

Encourageant l’inclusion et la diversité sous toutes ses formes, Christian Dior Couture s'est engagé depuis 2013 dans une démarche en faveur de l'emploi des personnes en situation de handicap, et de la valorisation de toutes les singularités.
