Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title

Senior Data Analyst

Location

Brno, Czech Republic

About Us

Ingersoll Rand is a global market leader with a broad range of innovative and mission-critical air, fluid, energy, and medical technologies, providing services and solutions to increase industrial productivity and efficiency.

Job Summary

As a member of Ingersoll Rand’s Internal Audit Department, the Internal Audit Sr. Data Analyst is primarily responsible for assisting the execution of internal audit work through developing repeatable data pipelines and analytics over areas frequently audited, administering audit technology (including reporting), and participating in special projects under the supervision of the Internal Audit Technology Enablement Lead.

Responsibilities


Assist in the risk assessment process by pulling and preparing data.
Build and regularly refresh data pipelines over commonly tested areas
Build and maintain analytic logic and visuals used for audit procedures
Identify opportunities, in partnership with audit team members, to improve efficiencies and effectiveness in SOX and operational audit testing
Own and maintain the analytics library, including versioning, documentation, and reuse across audits and SOX
Assist with the development and use of AI for the Internal Audit department to improve efficiencies, quality of work, and identification of risk
Perform training for audit teams on analytic logic and tool usage, including development of training documentation and standard work
Assist with access, changes, and reporting for audit GRC
Assist in fraud investigations, data-rich audits, department projects, and other projects as needed
Interface with external auditors as needed to provide information and documentation


Basic Qualifications


2-4 years of experience in a data analyst position, developing analytics
Proficient in common analytic programming languages (SQL, Python, R)
Bachelor’s degree related to Accounting, Analytics, or Information Technology
Experience using data prep tools (Alteryx, KNIME, SAS) or visualization tools (Tableau, QlikView, Power BI)
Must be fluent in English


Travel & Work Arrangements/Requirements


Hybrid model, up to 50% home office
International travel up to 10–15%


Key Competencies


2-4 years of experience in a data analyst position, developing analytics
Proficient in common analytic programming languages (SQL, Python, R)
Must be fluent in English


What We Offer


Motivating salary and annual bonuses
Meal vouchers
Equity grant (company shares)
Flexipass vouchers 750 CZK or Multisport Card for free
5 weeks of holiday, sick days, Volunteering day
Celebrate your birthday (day off)
Flexible working hours
Possibility of Home Office
Contribution to language/professional courses 6000 CZK
Contribution to pension and life insurance
Mobile service employee program
Pleasant and modern working environment close to the city centre
Refreshment at the workplace for free
Employee assistance program
LinkedIn Learning free access

Title: Data Management Officer (DMO) - H/F
Description:
Description Du Poste

Data Management Officer (DMO) SQL // H/F

🎯 Votre rôle

Vous rejoignez le Crédit Agricole d’Île-de-France dans un contexte clé : la création du Data Management Office (DMO).

Ici, tout est à construire.

L’objectif est clair : unifier, structurer et fiabiliser la donnée pour en faire un socle commun utilisé par l’ensemble des métiers de la Caisse Régionale.

Votre rôle est central. Vous n’êtes pas là que pour produire de la donnée au sens technique, mais pour définir la manière dont elle doit exister, être organisée et utilisée.

Vous intervenez au croisement de la gouvernance, des métiers et de la data, dans un environnement bancaire exigeant, notamment au regard des enjeux réglementaires (BCBS 239).

Vous contribuez directement à faire de la donnée un actif stratégique, fiable et piloté.

🧩 Vos missions

Votre quotidien s’articule autour de plusieurs dimensions complémentaires :

🔹 Gouvernance de la donnée

Vous définissez et structurez les règles de gestion de la donnée. Vous posez un cadre clair sur les méthodes de calcul, les référentiels et les usages afin de garantir une cohérence globale à l’échelle de l’organisation.

🔹 Qualité & fiabilité des données

Vous analysez les données existantes, identifiez les écarts et mettez en place des règles de contrôle. Vous pilotez des indicateurs de qualité et inscrivez la donnée dans une logique d’amélioration continue.

🔹 Structuration & modélisation

À partir de données brutes, vous êtes capable de :


recréer des tables pertinentes
redéfinir des logiques de calcul
organiser et ordonnancer les flux
construire des référentiels métiers fiables


Vous travaillez dans des environnements SQL, avec une logique de structuration et non de développement pur.

La compétence en développement SQL est impérative pour votre réussite en poste

🔹 Pilotage de projets data

Vous intervenez comme un véritable chef de projet sur les sujets data :


compréhension des besoins métiers
cadrage des usages
priorisation des sujets
coordination avec les équipes techniques


Vous assurez la bonne mise en œuvre des évolutions dans une logique de time to market.

🔹 Interaction métiers & accompagnement

Vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié des métiers sur les sujets data. Vous les accompagnez dans la compréhension, l’utilisation et l’appropriation des données mises à disposition.

🔹 Conformité & réglementation

Vous intégrez les contraintes réglementaires dans vos travaux, notamment :


BCBS 239
exigences de traçabilité
qualité et auditabilité des données


Profil recherché

🧠 Votre profil

Vous disposez d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique ou équivalent, et d’une expérience significative dans des environnements data structurants.

Vous maîtrisez les enjeux de gouvernance de la donnée, qualité et référentiels.

Vous êtes un développeur SQL senior et vous souhaitez donner un coup d’accélérateur à votre carrière.

Vous comprenez les enjeux réglementaires liés à la data, notamment en environnement bancaire.

Vous savez faire le lien entre des enjeux métiers concrets et des problématiques de structuration de la donnée.

🧬 Votre posture

Ce poste s’adresse à une personne qui aime structurer, clarifier et construire.

Vous êtes rigoureux, capable de poser un cadre là où il n’existe pas encore, tout en avançant dans un environnement en construction.

Vous avez une vraie capacité à prendre du recul, à analyser des situations complexes et à proposer des solutions concrètes.

Vous êtes à l’aise dans la collaboration transverse et savez embarquer des interlocuteurs variés.

Vous aimez les environnements exigeants, où l’impact est réel et visible.

🌱 Pourquoi rejoindre ce projet

Vous participez à l'expansion d’un Data Management Office, avec un rôle structurant et visible.

Vous intervenez sur des sujets à fort enjeu stratégique pour la banque.

Vous contribuez directement à transformer la manière dont la donnée est utilisée, pilotée et valorisée.

C’est un terrain de jeu rare pour quelqu’un qui veut construire, structurer et avoir un impact durable.

Vous définissez et structurez les règles de gestion de la donnée. Vous posez un cadre clair sur les méthodes de calcul, les référentiels et les usages afin de garantir une cohérence globale à l’échelle de l’organisation.

🔹 Qualité & fiabilité des données

Vous analysez les données existantes, identifiez les écarts et mettez en place des règles de contrôle. Vous pilotez des indicateurs de qualité et inscrivez la donnée dans une logique d’amélioration continue.

🔹 Structuration & modélisation

À partir de données brutes, vous êtes capable de :

  • recréer des tables pertinentes
  • redéfinir des logiques de calcul
  • organiser et ordonnancer les flux
  • construire des référentiels métiers fiables

Vous travaillez dans des environnements SQL, avec une logique de structuration et non de développement pur.

La compétence en développement SQL est impérative pour votre réussite en poste

🔹 Pilotage de projets data

Vous intervenez comme un véritable chef de projet sur les sujets data :

  • compréhension des besoins métiers
  • cadrage des usages
  • priorisation des sujets
  • coordination avec les équipes techniques

Vous assurez la bonne mise en œuvre des évolutions dans une logique de time to market.

🔹 Interaction métiers & accompagnement

Vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié des métiers sur les sujets data. Vous les accompagnez dans la compréhension, l’utilisation et l’appropriation des données mises à disposition.

🔹 Conformité & réglementation

Vous intégrez les contraintes réglementaires dans vos travaux, notamment :

  • BCBS 239
  • exigences de traçabilité
  • qualité et auditabilité des données

Profil recherché

🧠 Votre profil

Vous disposez d’une formation supérieure en data, informatique ou équivalent, et d’une expérience significative dans des environnements data structurants.

Vous maîtrisez les enjeux de gouvernance de la donnée, qualité et référentiels.

Vous êtes un développeur SQL senior et vous souhaitez donner un coup d’accélérateur à votre carrière.

Vous comprenez les enjeux réglementaires liés à la data, notamment en environnement bancaire.

Vous savez faire le lien entre des enjeux métiers concrets et des problématiques de structuration de la donnée.

🧬 Votre posture

Ce poste s’adresse à une personne qui aime structurer, clarifier et construire.

Vous êtes rigoureux, capable de poser un cadre là où il n’existe pas encore, tout en avançant dans un environnement en construction.

Vous avez une vraie capacité à prendre du recul, à analyser des situations complexes et à proposer des solutions concrètes.

Vous êtes à l’aise dans la collaboration transverse et savez embarquer des interlocuteurs variés.

Vous aimez les environnements exigeants, où l’impact est réel et visible.

🌱 Pourquoi rejoindre ce projet

Vous participez à l'expansion d’un Data Management Office, avec un rôle structurant et visible.

Vous intervenez sur des sujets à fort enjeu stratégique pour la banque.

Vous contribuez directement à transformer la manière dont la donnée est utilisée, pilotée et valorisée.

C’est un terrain de jeu rare pour quelqu’un qui veut construire, structurer et avoir un impact durable.

Title: MONITOR/A SALUD MENTAL (UME)
Description:
Inicio del proceso de selección: 09/06/2026 finalización: 18/06/2026

Fecha límite de inscripción: 15/06/2026

Número de plazas: 1

Requisitos mínimos

Se valorarán formaciones: - Técnico en Integración Social. - Técnico en Atención a Personas en Situación de Dependencia / Auxiliar de Enfermería (con experiencia en salud mental). - Estudios universitarios en curso o finalizados en Terapia Ocupacional, Trabajo Social o Psicología (con enfoque práctico/ocupacional).

Requisitos deseados

Formación y/o experiencia en el ámbito de la salud mental.

Experiencia mínima

No Requerida

Descripción

¿Te motiva acompañar a las personas en su proceso de recuperación y crecimiento personal? En la Unidad de Media Estancia de Salud Mental de Fundación Rey Ardid buscamos profesionales comprometidos, con vocación de apoyo y capacidad para generar entornos seguros, favoreciendo la autonomía, la participación y el bienestar de las personas atendidas. ¿CUÁLES SERÁN TUS FUNCIONES? - Apoyo a pacientes en sus Planes Individuales de Rehabilitación. Seguimiento y participación de las actividades pautadas. - Acompañamiento a los pacientes al exterior - Coordinar las actividades de la vida diaria con los pacientes - Cumplimentar documentos sobre los planes de fin de semana y supervisar su cumplimiento - Administración de las pautas individuales del tabaco y control economico según el plan individual ¿QUÉ OFRECEMOS? - Tres vacantes con CONTRATO TEMPORAL - Jornada completa. Mayoritariamente de Tarde y fines de semana, con mañanas y puntualmente alguna noche. - Trabajo en equipo multidisciplinar. - Salario según Convenio Establecimientos Sanitarios Privados de Aragón.

Title: Trainee AI Transformation & Data Science (m/w/d) International Graduate Program - Start November 202
Description:
Unternehmen: Vaillant GmbH Standort: Remscheid-DEU Land: Deutschland (DE) Marke: Vaillant Group

Vertragstyp: Unbefristet Arbeitszeit: Vollzeit

Was wir gemeinsam bewegen

Internationaler Karriereeinstieg - Gestalten Sie mit uns und unseren Kunden die Wärmewende! Im 24‑monatigen International Graduate Program mit Start zum 1. November 2026 (frühestens 01.09.2026) übernehmen Sie eine zentrale Rolle in der Weiterentwicklung unserer AI-Initiativen. Sie bewegen sich an der Schnittstelle von Technologie, Organisation und Menschen und tragen dazu bei, Künstliche Intelligenz nachhaltig im Unternehmen zu verankern. Dabei verbinden Sie analytisches Denken mit Kommunikationsstärke und schaffen echten Mehrwert für unsere internationale Organisation. So werden Sie bestmöglich auf eine garantierte Zielposition im AI Transformation Office vorbereitet.


In Ihrer Rolle als Trainee AI Transformation & Data Science (m/w/d) unterstützen Sie aktiv die strategische Weiterentwicklung unserer AI-Initiativen und deren nachhaltige Verankerung im Unternehmen
Ein Schwerpunkt Ihrer Tätigkeit liegt auf der Weiterentwicklung unserer AI-Kommunikation sowie der Unterstützung bei der Umsetzung zielgruppenspezifischer Trainingsformate
Gemeinsam mit dem AI Transformation Office gestalten Sie die Weiterentwicklung der AI-Ambassador-Rollen und begleiten diese insbesondere beim Best Practice Sharing sowie bei der Umsetzung von AI-Initiativen in Funktionen und Regionen
Durch die Übersetzung zentraler AI-Trainingsinhalte in funktions- und regionsspezifische Anforderungen leisten Sie einen entscheidenden Beitrag zur praktischen Anwendung von Künstlicher Intelligenz
Während Ihrer Trainee-Stationen – unter anderem im AI Transformation Office in Remscheid sowie im AI Team in Katowice (Auslandsstation) – erhalten Sie tiefgehende Einblicke in unsere internationale AI-Transformation
In weiteren Einsätzen in ausgewählten Funktionen bringen Sie AI gezielt in die Anwendung und unterstützen dort, wo der Bedarf an Integration und Transformation besonders hoch ist


Was uns gemeinsam erfolgreich macht


Qualifikation: Sie verfügen über ein überdurchschnittlich abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium oder stehen kurz vor dem Abschluss, idealerweise Master, z. B. in Data, Technologie, Wirtschaft oder Digitalisierung
Erfahrung: Erste praktische Erfahrungen, z. B. durch Praktika oder Werkstudententätigkeiten im Umfeld von Data, AI, Transformation oder Projektarbeit, haben Ihr Interesse an diesen Themen bestätigt – idealerweise haben Sie vorab eine Ausbildung im IT Bereich absolviert
Know-how & Fähigkeiten: Sie bringen ein grundlegendes Verständnis für Künstliche Intelligenz und datengetriebene Zusammenhänge mit und haben Freude daran, komplexe Inhalte verständlich aufzubereiten und weiterzugeben
Berührungspunkte mit Trainings, Kommunikation, Change Management oder internationalen Projekten sind von Vorteil
Persönlichkeit: Sie zeichnen sich durch Neugier, Eigeninitiative und eine strukturierte Arbeitsweise aus; gleichzeitig gelingt es Ihnen, Menschen zu begeistern, unterschiedliche Perspektiven zu verbinden und Themen aktiv voranzutreiben
Sprachkenntnisse & Mobilität: Sie kommunizieren sicher auf Deutsch und Englisch und bringen Offenheit für internationale Einsätze und neue kulturelle Perspektiven mit


Worauf Sie zählen können


Vergütungspaket: Wir bieten unseren Tarifmitarbeitenden ein attraktives Vergütungspaket nach IG-Metall-Tarif NRW inklusive Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld sowie weiteren Sonderzahlungen auf Basis einer 35h/Woche
Hybrides & flexibles Arbeiten: Wir bieten unseren Mitarbeitenden die Möglichkeit zur hybriden Arbeit. Darüber hinaus besteht auch die Option temporär aus einem Land der Europäischen Union mobil zu arbeiten
Onboarding: Durch unseren klar strukturierten Onboardingprozess inkl. Onboarding App sowie unser International Graduate Program inklusive Auslandsstation bereiten wir Sie optimal auf das internationale Daily Business vor
Buddy: Im Rahmen des Programms steht Ihnen von Beginn an Ihr Buddy zur Seite, die Sie in allen Fragestellungen unterstützt
Networking: Nationale Networking-Events sowie Lern- und Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten ermöglichen Ihnen eine sehr gute Arbeitsatmosphäre
Karrierepfade: Wir bieten unseren Mitarbeitenden individuelle, nachhaltige und zukunftsorientierte Karrierewege an, die die Potenziale und Stärken jedes Einzelnen anerkennen
Auslandsstation: Während des International Graduate Programs ist eine Station im Ausland obligatorisch. Dabei können Sie unsere internationalen Strukturen und fremde Kulturen kennenlernen und so das große Ganze aus anderen Blickwinkeln sehen


Was uns besonders macht

Wir sorgen für ein besseres Klima. In jedem Zuhause und unserer Umwelt. Diesen Anspruch leben wir als erfolgreiches Familienunternehmen seit über 150 Jahren. Wir sind einer der weltweit führenden Anbieter von Wärmepumpen, digitalen Servicedienstleistungen sowie effizienten Gas-Heizgeräten und stellen energieeffiziente Lösungen bereit.

Nachhaltigkeit finden Sie bei der Vaillant Group überall. Im Büro, an den Produktionsstandorten und unterwegs. Dafür setzen sich täglich rund 16.000 Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter aus über 70 Ländern ein. Als internationales Team übernehmen wir Verantwortung – für unsere Zukunft und die unseres Planeten. Wir fördern Ihre persönliche Entwicklung, damit Sie mit Leidenschaft handeln und wir gemeinsam für ein besseres Klima sorgen können.

Werden Sie Teil der Vaillant Group und gestalten Sie mit uns die Zukunft des Heizens!

Wir fördern Chancengleichheit und lehnen jede Form von Diskriminierung ab. Wir schätzen die Einzigartigkeit jeder Persönlichkeit und laden ausdrücklich alle Menschen zur Bewerbung ein.

Ihr Kontakt:

Michelle Fuhrmann

+49 2191 18 2601

Title: Principal Data Analyst
Description:
Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start Caring. Connecting. Growing together.

The Principal Data Analyst is a senior role in the Clinical Solutions - Risk Adjustment Team and this role is accountable for developing and scaling advanced reporting, analytics, and AI-driven solutions that improve organizational performance. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, they drive efficiency and effectiveness through automated insights, modern tooling, and data-driven decision-making.

The successful candidate will analyze complex datasets to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and guide strategic actions, while continuously advancing analytics capabilities across teams. They will collaborate cross-functionally with various business and data analytics teams, have proven business analysis experience and the ability to prioritize tasks effectively and adapt to changing business needs.

In addition to having impact on a great team, you'll also discover the career opportunities you'd expect from an industry leader.

Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny office and telecommuting from a home-based office in a hybrid work model.

What You Will Do


Develop, implement, and scale advanced analytics, reporting, and AI-driven solutions to improve organizational performance and decision-making
Develop a deep understanding of data and business processes related to In Office Assessment
Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Research and develop innovative analytics to generate value for Optum and our clients
Use data expertise to analyze data and provide solutions to optimize the performance of operations and data processing tasks
Design data integration processes utilizing AI, automation, process controls, and monitoring tools to ensure data quality
Validate data pipelines and provide design inputs to build scalable data and analytics processes/data flows
Provide quality checks to ensure high standard and accurate data
Ensure data integrity, quality, and compliance through structured validation, monitoring, and controls


You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications

If you don't meet every single requirement, don't let that hold you back - we're just as excited about potential as we are about qualifications and experience, and you could be exactly who we're looking for.


Bachelor's degree (or higher) in data analytics, statistics, engineering, or a related quantitative field
Demonstrated extensive experience in complex data analysis using relational and non relational (NoSQL) databases, including mining large datasets and working with SQL, Python, Databricks, or similar programming languages
Demonstrated ability to drive data driven business solutions in complex data environments utilizing multiple data sources
Proven experience delivering end to end AI and analytics solutions, including data analysis, validation, reporting, and operationalization
Proven ability to drive change and influence stakeholders at all organizational levels through technical discussions and analytical insights
Demonstrated solid communication and presentation skills, with the ability to simplify complex data insights for non technical audiences
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt to changing business needs in a fast paced environment


Preferred Qualifications


Demonstrated knowledge of Risk Adjustment within healthcare or data centric roles
Proven experience mentoring or leading data analysts
Demonstrated knowledge of data architecture, ETL (Extract, Transform, Load), data warehouse design, and large scale data processing


What We Offer


Opportunities for professional development
Inclusive and supportive team culture
Key benefits: Private health insurance, wellness programs, matching pension contribution, lunch provided by the company, training opportunities, employee donations matching and others


Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.

  • Develop, implement, and scale advanced analytics, reporting, and AI-driven solutions to improve organizational performance and decision-making
  • Develop a deep understanding of data and business processes related to In Office Assessment
  • Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, translating findings into actionable recommendations
  • Research and develop innovative analytics to generate value for Optum and our clients
  • Use data expertise to analyze data and provide solutions to optimize the performance of operations and data processing tasks
  • Design data integration processes utilizing AI, automation, process controls, and monitoring tools to ensure data quality
  • Validate data pipelines and provide design inputs to build scalable data and analytics processes/data flows
  • Provide quality checks to ensure high standard and accurate data
  • Ensure data integrity, quality, and compliance through structured validation, monitoring, and controls

You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in.

Required Qualifications

If you don't meet every single requirement, don't let that hold you back - we're just as excited about potential as we are about qualifications and experience, and you could be exactly who we're looking for.

  • Bachelor's degree (or higher) in data analytics, statistics, engineering, or a related quantitative field
  • Demonstrated extensive experience in complex data analysis using relational and non relational (NoSQL) databases, including mining large datasets and working with SQL, Python, Databricks, or similar programming languages
  • Demonstrated ability to drive data driven business solutions in complex data environments utilizing multiple data sources
  • Proven experience delivering end to end AI and analytics solutions, including data analysis, validation, reporting, and operationalization
  • Proven ability to drive change and influence stakeholders at all organizational levels through technical discussions and analytical insights
  • Demonstrated solid communication and presentation skills, with the ability to simplify complex data insights for non technical audiences
  • Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt to changing business needs in a fast paced environment

Preferred Qualifications

  • Demonstrated knowledge of Risk Adjustment within healthcare or data centric roles
  • Proven experience mentoring or leading data analysts
  • Demonstrated knowledge of data architecture, ETL (Extract, Transform, Load), data warehouse design, and large scale data processing

What We Offer

  • Opportunities for professional development
  • Inclusive and supportive team culture
  • Key benefits: Private health insurance, wellness programs, matching pension contribution, lunch provided by the company, training opportunities, employee donations matching and others

Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes - an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.

Title: Listening Bar & Brewery Internship
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Internship: Help Build and Run Our New Listening Bar Concept 6–12 months | Copenhagen | €700/month + possible paid shifts

The Opportunity

We’re building something new in the heart of Kødbyen — a small, ambitious listening bar located right next to the Kombucha Inc. brewery.

The bar is already open, but we’re still shaping the space, the concept, the routines, the events, the atmosphere, and the way people experience it. That means there is a lot to build — and we’re looking for a hands-on intern who wants to be part of that journey from the inside.

If you’re curious about hospitality, music, events, branding, community-building, social media, and what it takes to turn a small bar into a living concept, this is your chance to get involved in almost every part of the process.

This internship is especially relevant for a student who needs a practical internship as part of their study programme.

Your Impact

Building the Bar & Concept

Be part of developing the bar’s identity, atmosphere, routines, and guest experience.

Help shape how the bar looks, feels, communicates, and operates day to day.

Support the team in turning ideas into practical systems, events, and experiences.

Contribute to building a place that feels welcoming, creative, and alive.

Music, Events & Community

Help plan, coordinate, and run events at the bar.

Support everything from listening sessions and DJ nights to tastings, community gatherings, and creative collaborations.

Assist with booking, planning, communication, setup, execution, and follow-up.

Bring ideas for new events, music formats, partnerships, and ways to activate the space.

Operations & Running the Place

Take part in the daily running of the bar, including opening routines, service preparation, stock, and general coordination.

Help improve workflows, systems, and routines as the bar develops.

Work closely with the team to make sure the place runs smoothly, while still leaving room for experimentation and creativity.

There may also be opportunities to take paid shifts in the bar alongside the internship.

Social Media & Communication

Help build the bar’s voice and presence on social media.

Create content around the space, events, drinks, music, people, and behind-the-scenes moments.

Support with posts, stories, event communication, newsletters, and simple campaigns.

Help us tell the story of a new listening bar being built in real time.

Business & Concept Development

Support the development of new ideas for drinks, events, collaborations, and guest experiences.

Research trends, local culture, relevant brands, potential partners, music communities, and new opportunities.

Help us understand what works, what people respond to, and how the bar can grow.

What’s In It For You

A front-row seat to building a listening bar and hospitality concept from the ground up.

Hands-on experience across events, operations, branding, social media, music programming, and community-building.

A startup environment where your ideas can become real quickly.

The chance to work closely with a small, creative, and ambitious team.

Experience that is directly relevant if you’re interested in hospitality, entrepreneurship, culture, events, branding, music, or food and beverage.

A monthly internship compensation of €700.

Possible paid shifts in the bar, depending on availability and fit.

Who We’re Looking For

Someone curious, hands-on, and excited about building things.

A student looking for a 6–12 month internship as part of their study programme.

Someone interested in hospitality, music, events, social media, culture, branding, or entrepreneurship.

A person who enjoys working with people and creating good experiences.

Someone organized enough to help make things happen, but flexible enough to work in a place that is still being built.

A collaborative mindset — we’re a small team, and we work closely together.

You don’t need to know everything already. But you should be ready to learn, take initiative, and roll up your sleeves.

How To Jump In

No need for a lengthy application. Just send us:

A short video introducing yourself, or

Your CV, and examples of relevant past work, projects, events, content, or ideas.

Let us know why you want to be part of building the listening bar — and what you would love to help create.

Note: This is an internship with a monthly compensation of €700. There may also be the possibility of taking paid shifts in the bar.

Title: Database Engineer
Description:
About Us

Ruby Labs is a leading tech company that creates and operates innovative consumer products. We offer a diverse range of opportunities across the health, education, and entertainment industries. Our innovative teams are driving the future of consumer-led products, and we're always looking for passionate individuals to join us. Learn more about our story at: https://rubylabs.com/about-us/

About The Role

We are building a high-scale payment orchestration system that processes millions of financial transactions every month across multiple payment providers, regions, and products.

Our platform operates at the intersection of payments, billing, subscriptions, analytics, and internal CRM tooling, where data correctness, performance, and reliability are mission-critical. As our transaction volume and customer base continue to grow rapidly, our database layer has become one of the most important parts of the system.

We are looking for a strong Database Engineer who will own, scale, and evolve our data infrastructure, ensuring it remains fast, reliable, and ready for long-term growth.

You will be joining a high-talent, fully remote engineering organization with strong backend, infrastructure, and product teams. We value ownership, technical excellence, and pragmatic decision-making. Engineers at RubyLabs work on real scale, real money, and real impact.

As a Database Engineer at RubyLabs, you will be responsible for designing, operating, and optimizing databases in a high-volume payments environment. This role goes far beyond basic CRUD operations — you will work with large datasets, complex queries, performance bottlenecks, migrations on massive tables, and analytics pipelines.

You will collaborate closely with backend engineers and product teams to ensure our data layer supports both transactional workloads and analytical use cases, including internal CRM search and observability.

Key Responsibilities

Database Ownership & Scalability


Own and operate AWS Aurora (PostgreSQL) in a high-load production environment
Design and evolve schemas for large transactional domains (payments, customers, subscriptions, events)
Implement and maintain:
Table partitioning strategies (time-based, tenant-based, hybrid)
Advanced indexing (B-Tree, GIN, partial indexes)
Query optimization and execution-plan tuning
Handle databases with millions of new records per month while maintaining predictable performance


Performance Optimization


Analyze and optimize slow queries and production metrics
Identify and resolve:
Lock contention
Vacuum / autovacuum issues
Connection pool saturation
Long-running or inefficient queries
Proactively prevent performance regressions as data volume grows


Search & Internal Tools


Design high-performance search solutions for large operational datasets used in internal CRM tools
Implement efficient search strategies
Balance flexibility, correctness, and performance at scale


Analytics & Logging


Work with event and log data pipelines, including Tinybird / ClickHouse-based analytics
Decide which data belongs in transactional databases vs analytical stores
Enable fast, real-time insights for product, finance, and operations teams


Reliability & Operations


Ensure data integrity and consistency in financial workflows
Define and monitor database health metrics (latency, replication lag, storage, IOPS)
Plan and execute safe schema changes and migrations on large tables
Participate in incident analysis related to data performance or availability
Work in backups, replication, and disaster-recovery strategies


Qualifications


Strong hands-on experience with PostgreSQL in production
Experience managing large databases (hundreds of millions of rows or more)
Solid understanding of:
Indexing strategies
Query planners and execution paths
Transactions, isolation levels, and locking
Experience working with AWS-based infrastructure, ideally AWS Aurora
Ability to independently investigate and resolve complex performance issues


Strong Plus


Experience in payments, billing, fintech, or financial systems
Familiarity with analytical databases (ClickHouse, Tinybird, or similar)
Familiarity with OpenSearch or similar, including data ingestion from PostgreSQL
Experience designing data models for multi-tenant systems
Understanding of event-driven architectures and data pipelines
AWS certifications (e.g., Solutions Architect, Database Specialty)


Location

Ruby Labs operates within the CET (Central European Time) zone. Applicants from any country are welcome to apply for the position as long as they are located within approximately ± 4 hours of CET. This ensures optimal collaboration and communication during working hours.

Benefits

Discover the perks of being part of our vibrant team! We offer:


Remote Work Environment: Embrace the freedom to work from anywhere, anytime, promoting a healthy work-life balance.
Unlimited PTO: Enjoy unlimited paid time off to recharge and prioritize your well-being, without counting days.
Paid National Holidays: Celebrate and relax on national holidays with paid time off to unwind and recharge.
Company-provided MacBook: Experience seamless productivity with top-notch Apple MacBooks provided to all employees who need them.
Flexible Independent Contractor Agreement: Unlock the benefits of flexibility, autonomy, and entrepreneurial opportunities. Benefit from tax advantages, networking opportunities, reduced employment obligations, and the freedom to work from anywhere. Read more about it here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nkrN76JlZkbKj9WSOhlT1_mni_CZeDkHdwfIjPXVwvk/preview?tab=t.0#heading=h.ndsdl4wapxtt


Be part of our fast-growing team and seize this excellent opportunity for personal and professional growth!

Interview Process

After submitting your application, we conduct a thorough review which typically takes 3 to 5 days, but may occasionally take longer due to the volume of applications received. If we see a potential fit, we proceed with the following steps:


Recruiter Screening (40 minutes)
Technical Interview (60 minutes)
Final Interview (60 minutes)


Life at Ruby Labs

At Ruby Labs, we move fast, aim high, and expect the same from our team. We’re not here to play small—we’re here to build, grow, and win. That means we look for people who are ambitious, driven, and ready to give their best every single day.

This is a place for individuals who thrive under pressure, embrace challenges, and see opportunity in every obstacle. If you’re hungry to achieve, motivated by impact, and want to grow at the speed of your own ambition, Ruby Labs offers the platform to make it happen.

Here, effort is matched with reward. We recognize those who go all in and deliver results, and we create space for people who want more—more responsibility, more growth, and more success.

Title: Μηχανικός AI, Synthetic Data Generation
Description:
wherewework.gr εκμέρους της:

Technology & Software Employers in Greece

Ψάχνεις για θέση Μηχανικός AI, Synthetic Data Generation στην Ελλάδα; Είσαι στο σωστό μέρος.

Οι ρόλοι AI και μηχανικής μάθησης είναι από τις θέσεις με τη μεγαλύτερη ανάπτυξη και τις καλύτερες αμοιβές στο τεχνολογικό οικοσύστημα της Ελλάδας το 2026, με ζήτηση συγκεντρωμένη στη την Αθήνα. Εταιρείες προϊόντων, fintech, ερευνητικοί οργανισμοί και διεθνείς εταιρείες με ελληνικές ομάδες μηχανικών αναπτύσσουν ενεργά AI δυνατότητες.

Για να προχωρήσεις, απλά ανέβασε το βιογραφικό σου στο wherewework, και θα σε βοηθήσουμε να συνδεθείς με πραγματικές αγγελίες Μηχανικός AI, Synthetic Data Generation από πραγματικές εταιρείες, συμπεριλαμβανομένης της θέσης που βλέπεις τώρα.

Έτσι λειτουργεί:


Ανέβασε το βιογραφικό σου μία φορά. Κάνε κλικ στο "Υποβολή" σε αυτό το παράθυρο.
Σε αντιστοιχίζουμε με πραγματικές θέσεις Μηχανικός AI, Synthetic Data Generation που ταιριάζουν στην εμπειρία σου.
Εσύ έχεις τον πλήρη έλεγχο. Πριν το βιογραφικό σου κοινοποιηθεί, θα σε ειδοποιήσουμε και θα ζητήσουμε την έγκρισή σου.
Μόνο εγκεκριμένες αιτήσεις αποστέλλονται στους εργοδότες.


Για θέσεις Μηχανικός AI, Synthetic Data Generation στην Αθήνα, οι μισθοί κυμαίνονται συνήθως από 2,000 EUR έως 4,500 EUR μεικτά τον μήνα, με μέσο μεικτό μισθό περίπου 3,000 EUR. Εξειδίκευση σε Synthetic Data Generation, μαζί με άπταιστα αγγλικά και ισχυρό portfolio ή GitHub προφίλ, είναι οι κυριότεροι παράγοντες μισθού.

Όσο είσαι εδώ, εξερεύνησε το wherewework για να ανακαλύψεις πώς είναι πραγματικά η εργασία στις κορυφαίες εταιρείες τεχνολογίας και λογισμικού στην Ελλάδα, μέσα από εμπειρίες συνεντεύξεων, αξιολογήσεις εργαζομένων και κοινοτική ανατροφοδότηση.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - Experimentation & Analytics
Description:
What makes Cognizant Netcentric a great fit for you?

At Cognizant Netcentric, we create memorable digital experiences for the worlds leading brands. We are a diverse, global community of 1,500+ Adobe experts working across countries, cultures, and technologies.

Our inclusive culture blends the entrepreneurial spirit of a startup with the stability, resources, and learning opportunities of a global organisation.

With Holacracy at our core, every individual is empowered to make an impact.

About The Role

Want to shape how millions of users experience digital products through data and experimentation?

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to lead A/B testing and data-driven decision making across the digital user journey. You'll operate at the intersection of product, business, and data, driving measurable impact on customer experience, engagement, and conversion.

What You'll Do


Lead and scale A/B testing and experimentation frameworks
Partner closely with Product Owners and stakeholders to drive decisions
Turn complex data into clear insights and compelling stories
Contribute to the full lifecycle of data-driven digital products, from the initial ideation stage through to development and performance analysis
Define tracking and measurement strategies across web & app
Own reporting and dashboards for product performance
Drive alignment and evangelize data-driven thinking



What you bring


5–7+ years in Digital Analytics / Data Analysis
Strong expertise in experimentation & hypothesis-driven testing
Advanced SQL skills (BigQuery / Databricks preferred)
Hands-on experience with GA4, tag management (GTM / Tealium)
Experience with Tableau, Looker, Power BI or similar
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Comfortable in fast-paced environments



Nice to have


Experience with Monetate or similar A/B testing tools
Knowledge of CDP, personalization
Background in Travel, Retail or eCommerce



At Cognizant Netcentric you get:


Permanent contract
30 working days of vacation
Flexible working hours
Recognition bonus system
Private health insurance
Apple work equipment
WFH equipment + welcome package
WFH monthly expenses compensation
Access to Udemy courses
Adobe certification
Sponsored gym membership
Tax-free benefits (ticket restaurant, kindergarten and transport)
A dynamic and international team
Everyone has a voice; we are a Holacracy organisation

Title: Data Engineer
HTML:
At Axians, we value talent, not labels.

We believe in a culture of inclusion, where everyone has a place and all applications are considered based on merit, without discrimination.

This is your opportunity to join an international group with a project that needs you to help meet the challenges of digital transformation.

💻 THE ROLE

We are looking for a #TechTalent to work as a Data Engineer for a national project.

🙂 YOUR DAY TO DAY

  • Design high-performance data pipelines (ETL) to feed a DWH using state of the art data engineering techniques according to business specifications;
  • Implement flow automation through CI/CD pipelines and end-to-end release of artifacts across environments;
  • Design data models and schemas as well as providing architectural guidance and recommendations according to traditional BI best practices;
  • Provide end-users with support on the handling of Dataiku and Tableau, with support on the implementation of best practices;
  • Coach and guidance towards junior team members, as well as stakeholders.

💡 What We're Looking For

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business Intelligence or other relevant area;
  • Relevant professional experience as a data engineer is mandatory (5-7 years);
  • Knowledge of ETL and / or ELT data integration processes (Alteryx or Talend, for example) is mandatory;
  • Proficiency in SQL is mandatory;
  • Proficiency in Python and / or PySpark is mandatory;
  • Proficiency in Apache Nifi, Dataiku and / or Tableau is a plus;
  • Previous banking experience is a plus;
  • Knowledge of credit risk is a plus;
  • Knowledge of Agile methodology and JIRA is a plus;
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, Word).
  • Fluency in English (written & spoken), proficiency in French and Portuguese is a plus.

⭐ What You Can Expect From Us

Stable professional career

Working with Axians projects becomes an asset to those of us who want to offer stability through a secure career.

Prospects for career advancement

You can access training and new certifications that will further elevate your skills. The 360° evaluation from managers and peers will also allow you to evolve.

Work Life Balance

By collaborating with Axians, you will be able to reconcile work and family requirements without neglecting either area.

Attractive, Evolving Compensation

When working with Axians, you will have access to performance bonuses in addition to an attractive salary pack (base salary + benefits). You are also entitled to health insurance and an employee assistance program for you and your family (Psychological, Legal, Psychosocial, and Financial support).

Do you identify with this position?

If you think this position is the right fit for you and you have the #TechTalent we are looking for, send us your resume, preferably in English and in Europass format, to recrutamento.axianspt@axians.com with the subject “DE/TP/DB”.

Learn more about Axians

Title: Analyst, Data Management
Description:
Description

Join a fast-growing company that's transforming the industry! Financial Recovery Technologies (FRT) is a technology-based services firm that helps institutional investors such as hedge funds, mutual funds, custodians, and the like identify, file claims, and collect funds made available in securities class action settlements globally, and our best-in-class people and technology have made FRT a market leader.

As an Analyst on the Client Data Management Team, you will:


Quickly and accurately extract, load and transform client data
Use ETL tools to load FRT client data
Develop SQL programming/scripting experience, specifically in Oracle SQL
Be a source of new business requirements that can be used to automate data loading processes
Identify and communicate data loading issues
Quickly synthesize issues for colleagues to look at/resolve
Document gaps in existing processes
Verify quality of client data through existing processes
Build strong relationships with peers across corporate functions, including:
Data Integration and Client Services professionals, who you’ll work with to provide feedback on imported client data
Operations professionals, who will rely on your ETL skills to file claims, remediate deficiencies, and remit payments
Product and technology teams, who will count on you to serve as a QA resource as they develop technology to support operational requirements

Our Ideal Candidate Has


2-5 years of professional experience in an ETL role using SQL;
A Bachelor’s degree in a Business or Quantitative field (or 1-2 additional years of experience in a data-driven role);
Knowledge of database and data storage concepts;
A willingness to work with messy data;
Self-direction and detail orientation, with strong analytical, problem solving, and creative thinking skills;
A team-first approach, prioritizing collaboration over credit, and interested in building strong relationships with peers across corporate functions;
The mind of a planner, identifying and analyzing problems, proposing solutions, and anticipating future problem areas before initiating action;
Intellectual curiosity, with a demonstrated commitment to continuous growth; and
Of course, a desire to join a growing company with a vibrant, entrepreneurial culture, dedicated to being the top provider in the class action recovery space.


What Frt Offers


Competitive salary and bonus ($60,000-$80,000 annual base salary)
Health, dental, vision
401k (with company match)
Income protection plans (life, accidental death and dismemberment, short- and long-term disability) and access to a suite of voluntary benefits
Fun, smart, diverse colleagues
We are a hybrid company; in office, we offer:
Close to public transit (walking distance to Wellington T on Orange Line)
Free drinks and snacks
Free parking onsite
Free access to onsite gym

THIS POSITION WILL BE BASED IN OUR MEDFORD, MA HEADQUARTERS. LOCAL CANDIDATES ONLY, PLEASE.

AGENCIES: WE ARE NOT WORKING WITH AGENCIES ON THIS REQUISITION.

FRT provides equal employment opportunities by recruiting, hiring, training and promoting applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, age, ancestry, sexual orientation, genetics, pregnancy, marital or partnership status, gender identity/expression, disability, handicap, status as a victim of domestic violence or other similar offenses, consumer credit history, unemployment, military obligations, veteran status or any other category protected by law.

FRT is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the application or employment process, please let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

Title: Data Specialist
Description:
VIA is making an impact, and so can you.

At VIA, our mission is to make communities cleaner, safer, and more equitable. We believe that by working across organizational boundaries, we can achieve greater collective good than we can individually. VIA overcomes digital barriers to collective action by providing the world's most secure and simple data and identity protection solutions.

We are trusted by the U.S. Department of Defense and Fortune 100 companies around the globe to solve their toughest data and identity protection challenges. Using our Web3, quantum-resistant, passwordless technologies (19 issued patents), VIA protects data against theft, manipulation, and misuse.

As a Data Specialist at VIA, you'll play a pivotal role in the growth of our solutions. You are turning raw, complex data into the trusted, AI-enhanced intelligence that powers VIA's data products. Operating on a high-velocity Agile team with developers, data and modeling specialists, and client delivery professionals, your work directly empowers our customers to make high-impact decisions where precision, security, and clarity are non-negotiable.

Read more about our mission, team, and values here.




Requirements



In this role, you will: 

Understand the data and the domain

Partner with VIA's client delivery team and customers to translate domain knowledge into data infrastructure requirements, validate assumptions, and resolve data-related issues
Explore raw customer data to build a clear picture of files, columns, and characteristics (e.g. averages, expected ranges, trends, standard deviations) and make suggestions grounded in the data

Build and own data pipelines

Design and implement end-to-end, AI-enhanced  ETL/ELT pipelines — striving for maximum automation and self-healing — that move raw customer data into standardized relational and non-relational databases ready for the rest of the data science stack
Coordinate with internal stakeholders and customers when information is missing or discrepancies are found
Run quality control on data and data products through both automated tests and targeted manual review, and document the assumptions and decisions made along the way so the work stays traceable

Build AI-powered data products

Build AI into VIA's data products — automated insights, anomaly detection, AI-assisted data quality checks, and natural-language interfaces over operational data
Evaluate the quality and reliability of AI/ML outputs against domain expectations, and design the human-in-the-loop checks that keep our data products trustworthy
Deliver data-based products to external customers, including interactive data analysis and investigation platforms, data quality reports, statistical analysis, and visualizations that turn complex findings into clear stories

Improve the platform

Contribute to the continual improvement of internal tools for data cleaning and data quality assessment by identifying key data-related challenges that are ideal candidates for automation and AI enhancement


What you will bring to this role: 

3+ years of experience in a data-driven role or equivalent in data-related research projects
Bachelor's or Master's degree in science, mathematics, engineering, or a data-driven field
Competence in Python, R, or equivalent programming language
Competence in at least two of the following technologies:
Database technologies (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL)
Data science libraries (e.g., NumPy, pandas)
Data pipelining workflows and tools (e.g., Dagster, Airflow, dbt)
Cloud providers (e.g. AWS, Azure), including software development kits used to access data and services on these platforms 
Ability to translate complex data findings into clear, compelling narratives
Strong communication capability to decompose complex operational workflows into clear, repeatable steps that both teammates and AI tools can act on
Passionate about data integrity, with a proven track record of transforming raw inputs into high quality, trusted datasets 
A self-starter attitude and demonstrated ability to learn new technologies quickly
Experience in the following is a plus:
Generative AI tools (e.g. AWS Bedrock, LangChain)
Testing frameworks (e.g. pytest)


What does it take to be a successful VIAneer? Let's break it down, our VIAneers are:

Self-motivated and passionate about leaving everything they touch better than how they found it
Firm believers that people should love what they do and are eager to build a culture that enables them to do their best work
Creative problem solvers who respectfully challenge the status quo in the pursuit of excellence
People who lead discussions with curiosity and value diverse perspectives
Eager to explore new ideas, understand the power of feedback, and constantly seek opportunities to grow and develop their skills
Strong team players who thrive in collaborative environments and celebrate the success of others



Benefits



What can VIA do for you?

VIA offers competitive benefits, flexible work options, and individualized mentoring and growth opportunities. Here are just a few of our VIAneers' favorite perks:


Compensation: 
This role offers a salary range of $85,000 - $105,000 USD
401(k) plan with up to 5% employer contribution
Health: A fully funded, top-tier health benefits plan, including vision and dental coverage, fully covered from day one, for your whole family
Family Support: Up to 24 weeks paid parental leave, a 4-week paid ramp-back program, and a $10K family forming benefit (covering fertility treatments, adoption, and surrogacy)
Time off: Flexible Vacation Policy with no set annual limit or accrual period, Summer Fridays, and an extended holiday period in December
Remote work: Ability to enjoy the best of both worlds with flexibility to work from home as needed, as well as access to four well-located offices, designed for collaboration and stocked with everything you could need
Opportunities to work remotely from eligible locations for up to 2 months per year
Growth: Individualized growth opportunities, including internal and external mentorship panels, custom goals and feedback sessions, and/or access to learning and development programs, including VIA's unrivaled leadership program
A dedicated wellness advisor to help you navigate the programs and opportunities available at VIA
Transit: Benefits to support commuting costs
Connection: In-person events to foster team bonding and collaboration across different teams


Read more about our benefits and perks here.


Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Title: Data Support Analyst - Wealth Management (Johnston, RI)
Description:
Description

As a Data Support Analyst-Wealth Management, you will support Wealth Data Governance by monitoring data transmissions, investigating data quality issues, and assisting with the execution of data controls and documentation. This role focuses on hands‑on analysis, issue resolution, and collaboration with senior team members to ensure reliable data delivery across wealth platforms.

This position is ideal for an analyst who is detail‑oriented, eager to learn, and looking to grow deeper expertise in wealth data, governance, and data operations.

Key Responsibilities


Monitor data feeds and interfaces to identify data quality issues or transmission failures.
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies across Wealth Management systems with guidance from senior team members.
Execute data validation, reconciliation, and quality checks to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Assist with documenting data flows, feed specifications, transmission schedules, and issue logs.
Support incident tracking, root cause analysis, and remediation activities.
Partner with business and technology teams to clarify data requirements and support issue resolution.
Communicate findings, status updates, and questions clearly to team members and stakeholders.
Contribute data governance processes and control execution.
Support ongoing improvements to data quality, monitoring, and documentation practices.
Manage assigned tasks effectively while balancing multiple priorities.


Required Qualifications


Minimum 3 years of experience in an analytical, data, or operations‑focused role.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.

Title: SQL Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s a SQL expert and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


SQL Expert. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Paid Time Off. Outstanding time off benefits.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on customer data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Crown Linen Service, Inc is looking to add an ON-SITE Business Analyst to the team in Brockton, MA!

Join Crown Linen Service Inc as a Full-Time Business Analyst and immerse yourself in an environment where innovation meets excellence. Located in Brockton, MA, this onsite role offers the opportunity to tackle real-world problems and drive strategic initiatives. With a competitive pay range of $80,000 - $90,000, you will be rewarded for your analytical skills and contributions to our forward-thinking team. Experience the thrill of collaborating with passionate professionals dedicated to high performance and integrity in everything they do.

THIS IS NOT A REMOTE POSITION AND REQUIRES DAILY ON-SITE REPORTING TO OUR BROCKTON, MA LOCATION.

You will be provided great benefits such as Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), Life Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Account, Competitive Salary, and Paid Time Off. Don’t miss the chance to shape the future of our services while advancing your career in a supportive and dynamic workplace.

Make a difference as a Business Analyst

We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer


A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.


What You Bring


Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets and it is up to you how to get there.
A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings into executable business strategies.
A technically savvy approach.
Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape

As a new Business Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your business analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year

Make a difference as a Business Analyst

We are seeking a Business Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer

  • A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
  • An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
  • A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
  • A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.

What You Bring

  • Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
  • A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets and it is up to you how to get there.
  • A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
  • A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings into executable business strategies.
  • A technically savvy approach.
    • Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
    • A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
    • The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
    • Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
    • The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape
As a new Business Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your business analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Purple Carrot

Founded in 2014, Purple Carrot delivers clean, long-lasting energy through the power of plants. As the only company in America devoted entirely to plant-based eating, we have spent over a decade perfecting the balance of protein and fiber that only plants can provide. Offering chef-crafted, dietitian-designed meals delivered right to your door, we make plant-forward eating effortless, flexible, and endlessly exciting. Our menu spans quick weeknight dishes, occasion-worthy dinners, breakfast, lunch, snacks, and ready-to-eat meals.

Purple Carrot prioritizes taste and flexibility, offering no membership fees, the freedom to skip or unskip weekly deliveries whenever you want, and a no-pressure, cancel-anytime policy. We pride ourselves on doing plants better than anyone else - and we have the receipts to prove it.

Description

We are looking for an Analytics Engineer to join our mission-driven team. As an integral part of the Data team, you will join forces with the Senior Analytics Engineer in our continued effort to inspire people to eat more plants.

This position will play an important role in building and maintaining resilient, scalable data pipelines and company-wide data infrastructure that empowers Purple Carrot employees to better understand, manage, curate, and analyze the data most relevant to their work.

As the Analytics Engineer, you will be focused on maintaining and improving our data pipelines, supporting data systems and analytics, and helping develop rigorous data products within our modern data stack. This role will synthesize feedback from teams across the business and help translate data needs into reliable, well-documented solutions that allow us to move faster and gain better insights into how our business performs.

We greatly thank all applicants; however, only those under serious consideration will be contacted.

Location


This hybrid role is based in Dedham, MA
Our hybrid approach includes 3 days in-office each week, giving teams the best of both flexibility and collaboration. Many team members choose to come in more often - it's a great way to stay inspired and engaged with the team



On the Plate


Lead and contribute to the implementation and maintenance of end-to-end data pipelines and infrastructure, from ingestion through transformation and analytics
Develop, test, and optimize data models and data warehouse architecture with a focus on performance, scalability, and reliability
Ensure data quality through validation, monitoring, testing, and strong documentation practices
Implement and uphold data governance standards, including data dictionaries, lineage, access controls, and clear ownership of business logic
Build and maintain BI dashboards and reporting tools that help teams understand performance and make data-informed decisions
Support occasional ad hoc and exploratory data analysis to help answer business questions and identify trends
Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for data models, pipelines, reporting logic, and key data definitions
Collaborate closely with the Senior Analytics Engineer and cross-functional partners to execute on defined workstreams within an established data framework
Assist with ad-hoc projects and tasks as necessary



Main Ingredients for the Role


2-3 years of experience in the data space as a senior data analyst, data analyst, analytics engineer, data engineer, or equivalent role
Bachelor's degree in data analytics, computer science, information systems, business analytics, mathematics, statistics, or a related field, or equivalent experience
Able to commute to the Dedham, MA office 3 days per week
Proficiency in SQL and dbt for data transformation, automation, and modeling
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines using modern data stack technologies such as Git, dbt, Stitch, cloud data warehouses, or similar tools
Strong background in data quality assurance, governance frameworks, and best practices for documentation
Experience with BI and reporting tools such as Looker, Omni, or similar platforms
Ability to work independently on assigned workstreams while collaborating closely with a senior team member
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment



Extra Special Sauce


Experience working in or supporting a B2C subscription business model
Comfortable working within an established data framework while driving execution independently on defined workstreams
Strong curiosity and ability to translate business questions into clear, scalable data solutions
Excitement for working in a dynamic environment where data helps teams make faster, smarter decisions
You enjoy documentation, process improvement, and building systems that make work easier for others



Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this role is $90,000 - $100,00. Compensation is based on experience and qualifications, and the posted range is not a guarantee of any specific offer.

Garnish

We love our staff, and show it with benefits (including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, FSA, and life insurance), matching retirement plan, generous and flexible paid time off, Purple Carrot box delivery credits, professional development, and the opportunity to work in collaboration with teammates who are passionate about food!

Final Touches

We are committed to a safe and inclusive work environment, free of discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetics, national origin, ancestry, age, medical condition, mental or physical disability, handicap, veteran status, or marital status.
