Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on material data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities.

Title: Timberland: Digital Analyst (Americas)
Description:
At Timberland, we’re a force of nature. We’re a team that steps outside, works hard together, and moves the world forward. The brand is widely recognized as the arbiter of boot culture. With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community.

So, before we get to the job details, take a minute to learn a little more about us – our values and our culture. If you can see yourself working side-by-side with a team of fun, adventure-loving people, Timberland just may be the place for you.

To learn more about our values and our culture, visit Timberland Careers or www.timberland.com.

Digital Analyst (Timberland Americas)

What will you do?

The Digital Analyst will be responsible for leading performance reporting, full-funnel analysis, and digital insights for our North America eCommerce business. This role owns the digital analytics roadmap, partners cross-functionally to interpret performance trends, and influences strategies that drive KPI improvement. We’re looking for a data-driven storyteller with strong technical skills, a deep understanding of the digital funnel, and the ability to drive performance in a fast-paced environment.

Let’s break down that day-in-the-life a bit more.


Lead digital performance reporting for Timberland Americas—support the development, automation, and evolution of dashboards and KPI tracking tools across the full consumer funnel
Oversee insights and storytelling across daily, weekly, quarterly and seasonal reviews
Assist in authoring business requirements for any new reporting capabilities that need to be developed
Lead Monday Readouts—deliver a clear POV on business performance: what happened, why, and what action is needed next
Analyze end-to-end funnel performance, identifying improvement opportunities across consumer acquisition and retention, traffic, conversion, and average order value, while collaborating with cross-functional teams (Marketing, Content, Merchandising) to action against insights
Evaluate product, site paths, and campaign performance to uncover growth opportunities, reduce drop-offs, and optimize engagement, conversion, and personalization strategies
Assess funnel performance by page, category, and entry point, providing actionable recommendations to improve conversion rates and reduce bounce or abandonment
Lead the build and maintenance of KPI targets tied to financial goals—by channel and customer type
Own digital forecast accuracy—track and improve forecast-to-actual performance through collaboration with Planning and Marketing
Build forward-looking forecasting tools—connect seasonal assortment and customer acquisition plans to KPI expectations and long-range performance planning
Establish baseline reporting and monitor the effectiveness of loyalty program
Partner closely with the Operations team to report on the efficacy of personalization and optimization efforts
Grow Voice of Consumer programs with the goal of collecting insightful data, surfacing and reacting to rends to improve CX
Guide SEO strategy and execution—report on organic performance and support the implementation of optimization strategies
Recommend and integrate new tools or data sources to extend the team’s analytics toolkit
Elevate overall analytics capability


What do you need to succeed?

We all have unique skills that we bring to work and celebrate every day. For this role, there are foundation skills you’ll need to succeed and excel. Additionally, while formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in your 3-5 years of experience and professional achievements.

The foundation skills you will need in this position are:


Be the digital analytics subject matter expert—own/influence GA, Power BI, and VF platforms for the Timberland brand
Lead full-funnel performance reporting—from acquisition and engagement to conversion and revenue
Design scalable, accurate, and actionable reporting solutions in partnership with VF Analytics, Digital Technology, and business stakeholders
Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations that improve KPIs across traffic, conversion, margin, frequency and retention
Build a prioritized digital analytics roadmap—starting with gap analysis and evolving capabilities over time
Drive a culture of data-driven decision making, socializing insights and championing best practices
Balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution


What does success looks like?


KPI improvement across core digital performance metrics—traffic, conversion, average order value, and margin
Improved digital forecast accuracy and predictable performance against plan
High adoption of reporting tools that enable quick, data-informed decision-making
Strong team engagement and development
Trusted partnerships with Planning, Marketing, Content, and Merchandising
Clear and confident POV on business health and next-step actions


Now WE have a question for YOU.

Are you in?

Hiring Range:

$68,000.00 USD - $85,000.00 USD annually

Incentive Potential: This position is eligible for additional compensation awards that may include an annual incentive plan, sales incentive, or commission potential. Specific details of the additional compensation eligibility for this position will be provided during the recruiting and interview process.

Benefits at VF Corporation: You can review a general overview of each benefit program offered, including this year's medical plan rates on www.MyVFbenefits.com and by clicking Looking to Join VF? Detailed information on your benefits will be provided during the hiring process.

Please note, our hiring ranges are determined and built from market pay data. In determining the specific compensation for this position, we comply with all local, state, and federal laws.

At VF, we value a diverse, inclusive workforce and we provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants for employment will be considered without regard to an individual’s race, color, sex, gender identity, gender expression, religion, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, military or veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local laws. If you require accommodations during the application process, please contact us at peopleservices@vfc.com. VF will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals to the extent required by applicable law.

Pursuant to all applicable local Fair Chance Ordinance requirements, including but not limited to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, VF will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

With a rich heritage of craftsmanship and an eye toward the future, Timberland has a decades-long commitment to make products responsibly, protect the outdoors, and build community.

So, before we get to the job details, take a minute to learn a little more about us – our values and our culture. If you can see yourself working side-by-side with a team of fun, adventure-loving people, Timberland just may be the place for you.

To learn more about our values and our culture, visit Timberland Careers or www.timberland.com.

Digital Analyst (Timberland Americas)

What will you do?

The Digital Analyst will be responsible for leading performance reporting, full-funnel analysis, and digital insights for our North America eCommerce business. This role owns the digital analytics roadmap, partners cross-functionally to interpret performance trends, and influences strategies that drive KPI improvement. We’re looking for a data-driven storyteller with strong technical skills, a deep understanding of the digital funnel, and the ability to drive performance in a fast-paced environment.

Let’s break down that day-in-the-life a bit more.

  • Lead digital performance reporting for Timberland Americas—support the development, automation, and evolution of dashboards and KPI tracking tools across the full consumer funnel
  • Oversee insights and storytelling across daily, weekly, quarterly and seasonal reviews
  • Assist in authoring business requirements for any new reporting capabilities that need to be developed
  • Lead Monday Readouts—deliver a clear POV on business performance: what happened, why, and what action is needed next
  • Analyze end-to-end funnel performance, identifying improvement opportunities across consumer acquisition and retention, traffic, conversion, and average order value, while collaborating with cross-functional teams (Marketing, Content, Merchandising) to action against insights
  • Evaluate product, site paths, and campaign performance to uncover growth opportunities, reduce drop-offs, and optimize engagement, conversion, and personalization strategies
  • Assess funnel performance by page, category, and entry point, providing actionable recommendations to improve conversion rates and reduce bounce or abandonment
  • Lead the build and maintenance of KPI targets tied to financial goals—by channel and customer type
  • Own digital forecast accuracy—track and improve forecast-to-actual performance through collaboration with Planning and Marketing
  • Build forward-looking forecasting tools—connect seasonal assortment and customer acquisition plans to KPI expectations and long-range performance planning
  • Establish baseline reporting and monitor the effectiveness of loyalty program
  • Partner closely with the Operations team to report on the efficacy of personalization and optimization efforts
  • Grow Voice of Consumer programs with the goal of collecting insightful data, surfacing and reacting to rends to improve CX
  • Guide SEO strategy and execution—report on organic performance and support the implementation of optimization strategies
  • Recommend and integrate new tools or data sources to extend the team’s analytics toolkit
  • Elevate overall analytics capability

What do you need to succeed?

We all have unique skills that we bring to work and celebrate every day. For this role, there are foundation skills you’ll need to succeed and excel. Additionally, while formal education in a related field is great to have, we are most interested in your 3-5 years of experience and professional achievements.

The foundation skills you will need in this position are:

  • Be the digital analytics subject matter expert—own/influence GA, Power BI, and VF platforms for the Timberland brand
  • Lead full-funnel performance reporting—from acquisition and engagement to conversion and revenue
  • Design scalable, accurate, and actionable reporting solutions in partnership with VF Analytics, Digital Technology, and business stakeholders
  • Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations that improve KPIs across traffic, conversion, margin, frequency and retention
  • Build a prioritized digital analytics roadmap—starting with gap analysis and evolving capabilities over time
  • Drive a culture of data-driven decision making, socializing insights and championing best practices
  • Balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution

What does success looks like?

  • KPI improvement across core digital performance metrics—traffic, conversion, average order value, and margin
  • Improved digital forecast accuracy and predictable performance against plan
  • High adoption of reporting tools that enable quick, data-informed decision-making
  • Strong team engagement and development
  • Trusted partnerships with Planning, Marketing, Content, and Merchandising
  • Clear and confident POV on business health and next-step actions

Now WE have a question for YOU.

Are you in?

Hiring Range:

$68,000.00 USD - $85,000.00 USD annually

Incentive Potential: This position is eligible for additional compensation awards that may include an annual incentive plan, sales incentive, or commission potential. Specific details of the additional compensation eligibility for this position will be provided during the recruiting and interview process.

Benefits at VF Corporation: You can review a general overview of each benefit program offered, including this year's medical plan rates on www.MyVFbenefits.com and by clicking Looking to Join VF? Detailed information on your benefits will be provided during the hiring process.

Please note, our hiring ranges are determined and built from market pay data. In determining the specific compensation for this position, we comply with all local, state, and federal laws.

At VF, we value a diverse, inclusive workforce and we provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants for employment will be considered without regard to an individual’s race, color, sex, gender identity, gender expression, religion, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, military or veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local laws. If you require accommodations during the application process, please contact us at peopleservices@vfc.com. VF will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals to the extent required by applicable law.

Pursuant to all applicable local Fair Chance Ordinance requirements, including but not limited to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, VF will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

ButcherBox is a certified B Corp headquartered in Boston, and honored to be celebrating our fourth year in a row on Inc.'s list of Best Workplaces. At ButcherBox, we believe in better. That’s why we deliver 100% grass-fed, grass-finished beef, free-range organic chicken, pork raised crate-free, and wild-caught seafood directly to our members’ doors. All of our products are humanely raised or wild-caught and never given antibiotics or added hormones ever.

We’re working to build a world that’s better for all, and we’re inviting everyone to come along. For us, better means treating our planet with respect. It means improving the lives of animals and the livelihoods of farmers. It means never cutting corners when it comes to doing business. Ultimately, it means better meals, enjoyed together. Our team is made up of people who collaborate and support one another. We’re always looking for outstanding people to join our mission!

About The Role

We are seeking a Procurement Analyst to help us deliver the best quality meat and seafood to our customers and to create data assets and insights that make that possible at scale. This role requires someone with strong analytical skills who can work closely with stakeholders and vendors to build action-oriented reporting, deliver rigorous ad-hoc analyses, and meaningfully improve visibility into our protein supply chains.

An ideal candidate is deeply curious, a systems thinker, and takes ownership over the analytical needs of the teams they support. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who wants to see their work drive critical business decisions.

Responsibilities


Build and maintain actuals vs. forecast reporting across key protein categories, highlighting variances, root causes, and emerging trends to support proactive decision-making.
Develop data models and reporting to monitor the cost and quality of proteins throughout the supply chain.
Partner with finance and commercial teams to reconcile assumptions across forecasts, budgets, and actual performance.
Support yield and conversion reporting (e.g., raw to sellable yield, trim loss, waste) to improve transparency into cutter performance and efficiency.
Track key product performance metrics—such as repurchase rate, cannibalization, and seasonality—and deliver in‑depth analysis that enables teams to understand SKU‑level performance.
Support scenario analysis and “what‑if” modeling (e.g., demand changes, yield adjustments, cost inflation) to help the team assess risk and opportunity before issues materialize.
Build and continuously improve dashboards that track core procurement metrics (including inventory weeks on hand, product weights, and unit cost) to enable fast, confident decision-making.


Qualifications


3+ years of experience in an analytical role. Experience in procurement/supply chain/demand planning preferred.
Strong technical skills across analytics and data tooling (Preferred: familiarity with Databricks SQL, Developing Power BI, or similar cloud-native analytics environments).
Ability to synthesize complex information into clear, actionable insights and influence key decision makers.
Deep curiosity and desire to learn.
Bias toward action – strong visualizations are important, but analysis needs to drive change.
Ability and willingness to commute to our Watertown, MA office 2-3 times per week.


What Success Looks Like

In your first year, you’ll own our core procurement dashboards, have helped build at least one net-new data model that improves supply chain visibility and purchasing decisions, and be the go-to analytical partner for the procurement team.

Salary - $103,000-127,000

What We Offer

Our Values

We are a mission-driven company and strive to embody our values in every step of the process. ButcherBox is driven by relentless improvement, accountability, humility, and customer obsession. We want our workplace to be one of growth and learning where everyone can be safe/comfortable to show up as their authentic self to work. Anyone who loves our products and goals is welcome here! We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer aspiring to incite positive change in the pastures, the fulfillment centers, the office, and the world.

Benefits


Health: medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, an employer-funded HSA, and short & long-term disability benefits
Financial: 401(k) with generous employer match
Annual bonus based on company performance
Time off: unlimited paid time off policy, 20 weeks parental leave, an executive team that encourages a good work/life balance
Personal growth: Annual Learning & Development stipend, and access to a range of personal and professional coaching solutions through BetterUp
Food: a free Custom Classic ButcherBox each month and weekly lunch reimbursement
Others: reimbursement for wellness activities, an exceptional EAP, fertility benefits, life insurance and more


ButcherBox is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive environment at all steps in the hiring process. Every qualified applicant will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. We are happy provide reasonable accommodations during our hiring process. Please click here if you need to request an accommodation for your interview.

ButcherBox does not use artificial intelligence (AI) tools in any part of our hiring process. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Power BI Report Developer
Description:
As the Power BI Report Developer, you will be responsible for implementing and maintaining reports, ensuring accurate and timely delivery. You will collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements, develop Power BI reports, and optimize performance and user experience. This role involves strong analytical skills, proficiency in Power BI, and the ability to troubleshoot and enhance report functionality. This role offers an opportunity to stay current with Power BI advancements and contribute to continuous improvement in report development processes.




This full-time, salaried position is a hybrid role based out of our office in Peabody, MA.

US-based remote candidates will also be considered.




Moore is a data-driven constituent experience management (CXM) company achieving accelerated growth for clients through integrated supporter experiences across all platforms, channels and devices. We are an innovation-led company that is the largest marketing, data and fundraising company in North America serving the purpose-driven industry with clients across education, association, political and commercial sectors.




Check out www.WeAreMoore.com for more information.




Your Impact:

Strong understanding of data visualization principles and ability to translate business objectives into visual formats.
Work closely with the Senior Report Developer to understand project requirements and technical solutions.
Develop Power BI reports according to established standards and best practices.
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and provide technical expertise.
Perform testing and troubleshooting to ensure report accuracy and functionality.
Assist in optimizing report performance and user experience.
Promote best practices in dashboard design and data visualization within the team.




Your Profile:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, or a related field.
3+ years of experience developing Power BI reports.
Strong understanding of report development principles and best practices.
Proficiency in Power BI desktop and Power BI service.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work independently and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
Good communication skills and attention to detail.
Power BI certification preferred.
Familiarity with other report development tools, such as Tableau and Microsoft Reporting Services preferred.
Experience with Jira for workflow tracking preferred.
Experience using GitHub for report change management preferred.




This is a full-time, salaried position open to candidates in the US who are eligible to work without current or future sponsorship.




This position is a hybrid position for the Peabody, MA office.




The salary range for this role is $90,000 - 100,000, depending on skills and expertise.




How We’ll Support You:

Join the largest marketing and fundraising company in North America serving the nonprofit industry where we prioritize innovation and professional growth.
Collaborate with industry subject matter experts with over 5,000 employees across the enterprise.
To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including comprehensive healthcare, paid holidays and generous paid time off so you can have the time and space to recharge and pursue your other passions and be with the people you care about.
Moore is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst is a member of the Master Data (MD) Operations team and is responsible for ensuring Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, maintained, and governed in accordance with company data standards, internal control requirements, and defined Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This role supports accurate and timely data to enable efficient operations, reliable reporting, and sound business decision-making across the enterprise.

This position reports to the Master Data Manager and Master Data Supervisor and operates in a hybrid work environment in our Burlington, MA headquarters.

Position Responsibilities


Ensure Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, validated, maintained, and governed per company data standards, governance policies, internal controls, and SLAs.
Process new requests and change requests for master data objects including customers, vendors, and finance data (e.g., GL, Cost Centers, Profit Centers), ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and required approvals.
Maintain master data hierarchies, mappings, and reference data to support operational, financial, and reporting needs.
Review and approve master data updates and perform periodic data cleansing and data quality reviews.
Support business projects, system enhancements, testing, and validation activities related to master data and Master Data Management (MDM) solutions.
Partner with key stakeholder groups to understand business needs and deliver timely, accurate master data support.
Identify and implement process and system improvement opportunities; collaborate with manager and team to optimize end-to-end master data processes.
Ensure compliance with SOX and internal controls to mitigate operational, business, and financial reporting risk.
Participate in master data audits, governance forums, and cross-functional meetings.
Demonstrate core competencies including customer focus, accountability, problem solving, collaboration, and drive for results.



Business Support


Complete special requests and ad hoc analysis or reporting as requested.
Operate within internal and SOX controls by ensuring requests are processed and reviewed per established guidelines.
Escalate compliance failures and internal/SOX control issues to management on a regular basis.
Manage assigned workload to ensure SLA, productivity, and quality metrics are consistently met.



It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Total Rewards:


Salary Range: $62,500 - $72,000
Actual placement within the compensation range may vary depending on experience, skills, and other factors
Benefits, subject to election and eligibility: Medical, Dental, Vision, Disability, Paid Time Off (including paid parental leave, vacation, and sick time), 401k with company match, Tuition Reimbursement, and Mileage Reimbursement
Annual bonus based on performance and eligibility



Requirements:


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Information Systems, or related field, or 3+ years of equivalent work experience.
Experience with Customer, Vendor, and/or Finance Master Data and data standards applications.
Intermediate to Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g., VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, advanced functions).



Competencies


Strong attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to manage and prioritize high volumes of requests in a fast-paced environment
Effective written and verbal communication skills
Customer-service mindset
Ability to collaborate effectively and contribute to a team-oriented environment



Company Overview:

Keurig Dr Pepper (Nasdaq: KDP) is a leading beverage company with more than 150 owned, licensed and partner brands that meet a wide range of needs and occasions. Our North American refreshment beverage business holds leadership positions across carbonated soft drinks, water, juice and mixers with a portfolio of iconic brands such as Dr Pepper®, Canada Dry®, Mott’s®, A&W®, Peñafiel®, GHOST®, 7UP®, Snapple®, Clamato® and Core Hydration®. Our global coffee business spans more than 100 markets and includes the leading Keurig® single‑serve brewing system in the U.S. and Canada, along with powerhouse brands such as Peet’s, L’OR and Jacobs, and other regional coffee leaders. Our more than 50,000 employees aim to enhance the experience of every beverage and coffee occasion while making a positive impact for people, communities and the planet.

We strive to be an employer of choice, providing a culture and opportunities that empower our team to grow and develop. We offer robust benefits to support your health and wellness as well as your personal and financial well-being. We also provide employee programs designed to enhance your professional growth and development, while ensuring you feel valued, inspired and appreciated at work.

Whatever your area of expertise, at KDP you can be a part of a team that’s proud of its brands, partnerships, innovation and growth. Will you join us?

Keurig Dr Pepper is an equal opportunity employer and recruits qualified applicants and advances in employment its employees without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, disability or association with a person with a disability, medical condition, genetic information, ethnic or national origin, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by law.

A.I. Disclosure:

KDP uses artificial intelligence to assist with initial resume screening and candidate matching. This technology helps us efficiently identify candidates whose qualifications align with our open roles. AI does not make hiring decisions; all decisions throughout the hiring process are made by talent acquisition team members. If you prefer not to have your application processed using artificial intelligence, you may opt out by emailing your resume and qualifications directly to kdpjobs@kdrp.com in lieu of clicking Apply. In order for your application to be considered and opted out of AI, you must include the words "AI Opt-Out" and either job title and location or Job ID # in the email subject line in your email application.

In order for your application to be considered and opted out of AI, you must include the words "AI Opt-Out" and either job title and location or Job ID # in the email subject line in your email application.

Title: Data Visualization Analyst
Description:
Ocean Spray is hiring for a(n) Data Visualization Analyst! We’re a team of farmers, thinkers, creators, and doers. Whatever your title, whatever your role — it always comes back to this: we’re a farmer-owned co-op where everyone rolls up their sleeves to get the job done. Three maverick farmers started it all — and we’ve been making our own way ever since.

Role

The Data Visualization Analyst role is designed to help the commercial teams extract, summarize and visualize relevant past performance metrics to drive future performance. It requires a creative thinker who has the ability to transform ideas into actionable tolls that help teams deliver their objectives. This role will:


Work across multiple business teams learning how they operate, understanding insights they need to make decisions
Analyze information from multiple data sources, external and internal to identify business drivers that affect growth and efficiency
Design reports, tools and tracking mechanisms
Translate ideas into tangible output


A Day in the Life...


Delivering insights using compelling Power BI or SAC visualization techniques for business team.
Ability to translate business needs into actionable reporting and visualization thru rapid prototyping and storytelling.
Building models and designing experiments that seek to uncover insights that drive performance.
Analyzing complex data made of unstructured, semi-structured and structured datasets to drive insights.
Active participant in designing creative new ways to use data to help commercial team drive growth.
Work in an environment with internal IT, business teams and external vendors to design and scale work-flows, templates and dashboards .


What We Are Looking For


BA/BS in computer science or related field or equivalent relevant experience, preferably with a focus on data visualization
Minimum 5 years of related experience
Extensive experience with Data Visualization using tools like PowerBI (preferred) and Tableau
Demonstrate ability to work effectively with others within a complex, cross-functional and fast paced organization
Excellent communication. Ability to present ideas and insights to business stakeholders
CPG industry knowledge


Education

Bachelor's or University Degree (Required)

Work Experience

At least 3 Years of Experience

Annual Salary

$87,400 - $120,230

The base salary range information above serves as a guideline of the position’s typical hiring range. We value and appreciate what makes you unique and will consider a variety of factors when determining an offer. These factors include, but are not limited to, your skills and experience, external and internal benchmarks, as well as overall company considerations. Certain positions may be eligible for short-term and long-term incentive rewards. We also offer a competitive and comprehensive benefits program that supports all aspects of your health and well-being.

Benefits


Complete insurance package on Day-1 that includes a plethora of health and wellness programs
Health, Dental, and Vision insurance
Health savings account
Flexible spending account
Life and accident insurance
Employee assistance program
Telehealth services
1:1 health coaching
Supportive benefits for all the stages of your life
401(k) with up to 6% Company matching; additional potential discretionary match at year-end
Short-Term Incentive/Performance bonuses
Flexible scheduling options
Vacation pay, up to three weeks of time (pro-rated for your first year of employment)
Holiday pay for 12 holidays
Career development and growth opportunities
Tuition/Education assistance programs
Access to LinkedIn Learning
Scholarship programs for children of employees
Parental leave
Bright Horizons Family Solutions – Back-up care, tutoring, etc.
Adoption assistance
Bereavement leave
Up to $300 fitness reimbursement
Up to $300 massage reimbursement
Employee appreciation events
Employee discounts
Charitable giving

Who We Are

You might have our iconic cranberry juice in your fridge or have gotten into heated holiday debate about what’s better - canned or fresh cranberry sauce. But did you know that the hardworking people growing the superfruit in our products are 700 family farmers that own our cooperative? They entrust us with what is most precious to them to create new and innovative products that will delight consumers and grow this beloved brand today and into the future.

Team Members, Farmers, Consumers And Communities Alike--we Value What Makes Us Unique And Strive To Connect Our Farms To Families For a Better Life By Living Our Values


Grower Mindset – We embrace our grower-owners innovative spirit and heritage through confidence, learning and focus on the future.
Sustainable Results – Guided by purpose, we are focused on delivering results for our grower-owners.
Integrity Above All – We are ethical, doing the right thing for our grower-owners, customers, consumers and each other
Inclusive Teamwork – We build diverse and inclusive teams that strengthen our cooperative.


All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

For MA Applicants – It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. Any employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives.

WHOOP is hiring Product Analysts to embed across the product organization and serve as strategic analytics partners to teams across Healthcare, Algorithms, AI, Hardware, and other product areas. In this role, you will help define how WHOOP measures product success, uncover insights that shape roadmap decisions, and translate complex data into clear recommendations that improve the member experience. You will bring strong analytical rigor, product intuition, and intellectual curiosity to help teams make faster, smarter, and more impactful decisions with data.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product, Engineering, Research, Design, and cross-functional leadership to inform strategy and roadmap decisions with data.
Own the analytical narrative for your product area, illustrated by defining KPIs, refining measurement frameworks, and surfacing insights that drive action.
Support experimentation and feature launches end-to-end: from defining success metrics to post-launch analysis and iteration.
Build scalable, self-serve reporting & tooling that lets your stakeholders make decisions without waiting on you.
Develop deep context on your product domain and use it to ask sharper questions, challenge assumptions, and push the product forward.
Proactively surface insights and recommendations that go beyond the ask; identify opportunities others haven't seen yet.
Collaborate with a high-performance analytics team in a fast-paced environment where intellectual curiosity is the baseline expectation.


Qualifications


You've done this before: 2-6+ years in an analytical role where you owned business-critical metrics or initiatives. (We're hiring across levels; we'll figure out the right fit together.)
You use AI tools proactively and hold AI-assisted work to the same bar as your own.
You can take a vague question, wrangle the right data, isolate insights, and communicate actionable recommendations to stakeholders who aren't data people.
You're a strong storyteller: you know how to make a compelling, concise case for action.
You're technically sharp: advanced SQL is table stakes. Familiarity with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, ELT workflows) and visualization tools (Hex, Sigma, Amplitude, Looker) is a plus.
You have product sense: you think about the member experience, not just the numbers.
You're curious about how things work. Algorithms, hardware, health science: you don't need to be an expert, but you should want to understand.
You're a self-starter who thrives in ambiguity and doesn't wait to be told what to work on.


TEAMS WE'RE HIRING FOR

We’re Looking For Multiple Analysts To Work Across Our Product Teams. During The Interview Process, We'll Work With You To Find The Right Team Fit Based On Your Interests And Strengths


AI Product: The future of intelligent coaching and personalized insights powered by AI/ML.
Algorithms: Where the science meets the product; sleep, recovery, strain, and how algorithmic outputs translate into member value.
Hardware Product: The physical device experience; sensors, wearability, and next-gen hardware.
Healthcare Product: Building WHOOP's Health Operating System; health outcomes, clinical features, personalized wellness.


This role is based in the WHOOP office located in Boston, MA. The successful candidate must be prepared to relocate if necessary to work out of the Boston, MA office.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility

The WHOOP compensation philosophy is designed to attract, motivate, and retain exceptional talent by offering competitive base salaries, meaningful equity, and consistent pay practices that reflect our mission and core values.

At WHOOP, we view total compensation as the combination of base salary, equity, and benefits, with equity serving as a key differentiator that aligns our employees with the long-term success of the company and allows every member of our corporate team to own part of WHOOP and share in the company’s long-term growth and success.

The U.S. base salary range for this full-time position is $100,000 - $155,000. Salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within each range, individual pay is based on factors such as job-related skills, experience, performance, and relevant education or training.

In addition to the base salary, the successful candidate will also receive benefits and a generous equity package.

These ranges may be modified in the future to reflect evolving market conditions and organizational needs. While most offers will typically fall toward the starting point of the range, total compensation will depend on the candidate’s specific qualifications, expertise, and alignment with the role’s requirements.

Title: Business Analyst | Philippines
Description:
Description

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Business Analyst with strong experience in data analysis, reporting, and business process improvement. The ideal candidate will play a key role in transforming data into actionable insights, supporting decision-making, and driving operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct business process analysis to identify improvement opportunities and optimize workflows
Gather, document, and manage business and functional requirements
Design and develop interactive dashboards and reports to support data-driven decisions
Prepare and deliver business case presentations to stakeholders
Translate complex data into clear insights for both technical and non-technical audiences
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement analytics solutions and process enhancements


Requirements

Preferred Experience


Prior experience in Business Analytics within a BPO (Business Process Outsourcing) environment


Required Skills & Qualifications


Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
Strong experience with Power Query and Power Pivot
Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal)
Basic understanding of database structures, tables, and data relationships
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking abilities


Preferred / Nice-to-Have Skills


Experience in scheduling, forecasting, and capacity planning
Hands-on experience with Salesforce reporting (Classic & Lightning)
Ability to create and modify Salesforce reports
Experience in Zendesk report creation, including MAQLs
Familiarity with Microsoft PowerApps and Power Automate
Experience building or optimizing analytical/solving models


Key Competencies


Strong attention to detail and data accuracy
Stakeholder management and presentation skills
Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
Continuous improvement mindset


Peak Support and our Work-from-Home PLUS model

At Peak Support, we are dedicated to providing exceptional service to our clients and an exceptional work environment for our team members.

We don’t do this with ping pong tables or video games. We do it by creating a positive, encouraging and performance-driven culture that enables our team members to build rewarding, long-term careers. We’re proud that our Glassdoor rating of 4.5 is the highest in the Business Process Outsourcing industry.

Peak Support offers all the convenience of working from home, with the benefits of working for an established organization with an exceptional team and tremendous opportunities for growth. Peak Support has been 100% remote since day one, so we have built a culture and a set of processes that support the success of our remote team members.

Title: Data Analyst / Engineer, Artificial Intelligence & Technology
Description:
Position Requirements




Data Infrastructure & Engineering

Design, build, and maintain a centralized data lakehouse using Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Lake, and OneLake, consolidating data from Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, SharePoint, Chatham, and other source systems.
Develop and manage automated data pipelines that extract, clean, and load data from all source systems into a single, reliable layer accessible to Power BI, AI models, and future tools.
Execute daily, weekly, and monthly data integrity processes crucial for supporting quantitative models and portfolio reporting.
Optimize data gathering and validation processes for efficiency and scalability as the firm’s data footprint grows.
Ensure data governance and consistency across all integrated platforms, reducing reliance on stale exports and manual reporting.




Analytics & Reporting

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards for property performance, portfolio analytics, investment underwriting, and leadership reporting.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop data-driven narratives using visualization dashboards for internal asset operations and investor reporting.
Conduct research and curate datasets from internal and external sources; derive actionable insights to inform leasing, acquisitions, and capital planning decisions.
Provide financial analysis support across business units including investments, asset management, and accounting.
Translate complex data into clear, strategic insights that drive informed business strategies for senior leadership.




AI Data Layer & Model Integration

Serve as the data infrastructure owner for AI initiatives including Ask Bulfinch, automated deal memos, lease abstraction, and email triage—ensuring AI models have access to clean, well-structured Bulfinch data.
Integrate Azure OpenAI and/or Claude API with the firm’s data layer, enabling AI tools to query and reason over live portfolio information.
Collaborate with the external AI Strategist and AI Steering Committee on data requirements for new AI tool builds and model deployments.
Support Dataverse implementation for structured CRM-style records, deal tracking, and Power Apps integrations where applicable.




Cross-Functional Collaboration

Facilitate data capture through internal systems, enabling business owners across acquisitions, accounting, property management, and leasing to harness available information effectively.
Work closely with the IT Specialist on M365 administration, system access, security, and infrastructure to ensure a stable, secure data environment.
Support and actively contribute to Bulfinch’s AI Steering Committee, providing data infrastructure input on governance, initiative prioritization, and adoption best practices.
Collaborate with IT Specialist on technology vendor coordination and data providers including Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, and Chatham.




QUALIFICATIONS

Required

3–5 years of professional experience in data engineering, data analytics, or a related field—ideally within commercial real estate, PropTech, finance, or a data-intensive industry.
Proficiency in Microsoft database management tools including Fabric, Azure SQL, and OneLake; hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines across multiple source systems.
Strong command of SQL, Excel, and VBA for data transformation, querying, and automation.
Demonstrated experience with Power BI or Tableau for dashboard development and enterprise reporting.
Experience analyzing large, complex datasets with a focus on structure, integrity, and scalability.
Strong communication and presentation skills; ability to translate complex data concepts for non-technical stakeholders across acquisitions, accounting, and property management.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science, Statistics, Data Analytics, Data Science, or a related field.




Preferred

Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700) certification
Proficiency in Python, R, or similar data-centric programming languages.
Experience with relational databases and REST APIs for integrating third-party platforms.
Hands-on experience with Azure OpenAI, Claude API, or other LLM integrations for AI-powered applications.
Familiarity with Microsoft Dataverse and Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate).
Experience with CRE platforms such as Yardi, Argus, Juniper Square, MRI, or Chatham.
Basic understanding of accounting processes and familiarity with private equity, investment risk, or capital markets.
Working knowledge of commercial real estate fundamentals—asset classes, leasing, asset management, or investment underwriting.
Exposure to the PropTech landscape (VTS, Measurabl, CoStar, Placer.ai, etc.).
Graduate degree (MS in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field) a plus.

Title: Manager, Compensation Data and Analytics
Description:
Job Description

The Role

The Manager, Compensation Data and Analytics works in the Corporate Compensation team and assists with reporting and data visualization using a vast array of reporting tools and code base analysis. In this role, you will build robust ETL workflows in Alteryx, automate system administration with PowerShell, develop interactive Tableau dashboards, and utilize JavaScript to integrate analytics into our broader application ecosystem. You will bridge the gap between complex data extraction, automation, and visually compelling business intelligence


Assist in the development of reporting and analytic tools using Snowflake, Alteryx, Excel, Tableau, PowerShell, JavaScript and other Fidelity systems
Testing and ensuring the quality of core data, reports and tools
Defines, interprets and analyzes problems in terms of system requirements and suggests modifications to the system design
Partner with technology team to set up and improve existing data infrastructure
Assist with issue resolution processes, communicate project milestones to all levels of the Compensation organization
Creates complex data visualizations that will be used for executive presentations
Work with cross functional groups (technology and business unit HR) to support delivery of compensation tools and analytics.
Work with compensation data to uncover information and insights to help senior executives make decisions on a wide array of compensation programs including annual bonus, short-term and long-term incentives, as well as ongoing ad hoc information requests.


The Expertise And Skills You Bring

The role requires expertise with reporting tools such as Tableau, PowerBI and Alteryx. Additionally, coding experience with SQL, PowerShell, Javascript is helpful. The candidate must be able to translate business needs into technical requirements and turn it into actionable insights into our Compensation Programs.


College degree with 2-5 years of experience using reporting tools, relational databases, and creation of reports, and analytics.
SQL, Alteryx, Tableau, or other reporting/Data ETL tool skills required
Powershell, Javascript, Other coding experience preferred
Outstanding Microsoft Office skills, especially Excel and Powerpoint


Note: Fidelity is not providing immigration sponsorship for this position

The Team

The Compensation Data and Analytics team sits within the HR Function and supports HR leadership and the broader HR function. Our team analyzes the effectiveness of compensation programs for Fidelity associates. We provide executive decision support that enables us to attract and retain talent across all job roles. Within the HR function we provide data and insights to support the Compensation function and HR leaders allowing them to make the pay decisions throughout the year.

The base salary range for this position is $80,000-$153,000 per year.

Placement in the range will vary based on job responsibilities and scope, geographic location, candidate’s relevant experience, and other factors.

Base salary is only part of the total compensation package. Depending on the position and eligibility requirements, the offer package may also include bonus or other variable compensation.

We offer a wide range of benefits to meet your evolving needs and help you live your best life at work and at home. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage and emotional well-being support, market-leading retirement, generous paid time off and parental leave, charitable giving employee match program, and educational assistance including student loan repayment, tuition reimbursement, and learning resources to develop your career. Note, the application window closes when the position is filled or unposted.

Please be advised that Fidelity’s business is governed by the provisions of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, the Investment Company Act of 1940, ERISA, numerous state laws governing securities, investment and retirement-related financial activities and the rules and regulations of numerous self-regulatory organizations, including FINRA, among others. Those laws and regulations may restrict Fidelity from hiring and/or associating with individuals with certain Criminal Histories.

Title: Supply Chain Planning Analyst
Description:
Job Details

Description

Supply Chain Planning Analyst

Who We Are

At Harvey Performance Company, we're more than a team; we're a community. Our thriving, dynamic, and rapidly expanding organization values community, celebrates success, and fosters personal and professional growth opportunities.

The Opportunity

The Planning Analyst builds the data, dashboards, and KPI reporting that support Demand Planning, Production Planning, and inventory optimization at Harvey Performance Company. This is a newly created role for an analyst who can write SQL, build Power BI dashboards, and translate a planner's question into a query and a useful report. You'll work closely with our planning leadership and database team, with direct mentorship and support as you grow into the role. You'll also contribute to a new planning and inventory optimization system going in later this year. Rowley, MA preferred with one day per week onsite. Remote across New England considered for the right candidate.

In This Role, You Will


Build and maintain KPI reporting and dashboards for Demand Planning and Production Planning, covering service performance, inventory health, forecast accuracy, and planning execution.
Write SQL against ERP sources (Epicor P21, SAP, Kinetic) to produce repeatable, validated datasets that planners and buyers can trust.
Develop planning data models in Power BI that surface what matters and leave out what doesn't.
Help establish data validation routines and refresh cadences that create a single source of truth for planning data.
Support tuning of planning parameters (lead times, MOQs, safety stock) alongside Production Planning and Demand Planning.
Contribute to the upcoming inventory optimization and demand planning system rollout, including requirements gathering, field mapping, testing, and post-deployment KPI validation, with guidance from planning leadership.
Partner with IT and the database team on data access and reporting.
Build repeatable analytical workflows that reduce manual effort over time.


You'll Bring


2+ years in analytics, BI, or supply chain/planning analysis, ideally in a manufacturing or distribution environment.
Solid SQL skills, including joins and validation logic. You can write queries from scratch, not just modify existing ones.
Power BI experience building dashboards that operating teams actually use.
Familiarity with supply chain basics: PO flow, sales order flow, and planning master data (items, lead times, MOQs, safety stock), or a strong interest in learning them.
Exposure to an ERP system. Epicor or SAP a plus, but any major ERP works.
Ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions and work cross-functionally with IT and operations.
Clear written and verbal communication. You can explain a dashboard to a non-technical stakeholder and a data model to an IT partner.


Nice To Have


Exposure to a planning systems implementation (requirements, field mapping, UAT, post-deployment validation).
Familiarity with a modern demand planning or inventory optimization tool (Kinaxis, o9, Logility, Blue Yonder, OMP, RELEX, SAP IBP, or similar).
Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Analytics, Engineering, Finance, MIS, or related field.


Where You'll Work

Find yourself in the heart of Rowley, MA, where our modern facility resides. Experience the balance between vibrant professional growth at Harvey and the soothing, scenic lifestyle of this quaint North Shore town. We champion work-life harmony and invite you to be part of our journey.

What Harvey Provides


A fun, rapid-growing environment where new opportunities are regularly made available.
Opportunities for training and development, including tuition reimbursement.
A comprehensive benefits package, including PTO, paid holidays, health, dental, vision insurance, and a 401k match program, along with profit sharing.


The pay range for this position is $75K to $85K annually. This range reflects what the company reasonably expects to pay at the time of posting. Where an individual’s pay falls within the range will depend on their skills, background, and performance.

Title: Analyst Reporting & Analysis
Description:
Category/Area of Expertise: Data-Science & Analytics

Job Requisition: 504010

Address: USA-MA-Quincy-1385 Hancock Street

Store Code: ADUSA Distribution Services (5172986)

ADUSA Distribution is the distribution company of Ahold Delhaize USA, providing distribution services to one of the largest grocery retail supply chains in the nation. ADUSA Distribution fosters a culture that inspires and empowers our associates to always deliver exceptional service and value. Our team is supporting the supply chain network evolution to an integrated self-distribution model of the future, serving leading omnichannel grocery brands - Food Lion, Giant Food, The GIANT Company, Hannaford and Stop & Shop. To learn more about our company and the other supply chain companies of Ahold Delhaize USA, visit www.adusasc.com.

What You'll Do (Role Overview)

As an Analyst III, Reporting and Analysis, you'll play a critical role in shaping how data drives decisions across our Distribution & Transportation network.

In this role, you'll go beyond building reports you'll unlock insights, influence strategy, and help leaders understand what's happening across the business and what to do next.

You'll work with large, complex data sets, translate them into meaningful stories, and provide clear recommendations that help drive smarter, faster decisions.

Why This Role Matters

Data is at the heart of how we operate and this role helps ensure we're using it to its fullest potential.

As a trusted partner to leadership, you will:


Turn data into actionable insights that drive performance
Support key initiatives across Distribution, Transportation, and Supply Chain
Help improve the tools, reporting, and analytics that guide our business forward


Your work will directly impact operational efficiency, strategic priorities, and overall business performance.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Turn Data into Insights


Analyze complex data sets to uncover trends, risks, and opportunities
Connect insights to broader Distribution & Transportation and Supply Chain strategies
Provide recommendations that help leaders make confident, data-driven decisions


Build & Enhance Reporting


Design, create, and maintain reporting that is accurate, scalable, and user-friendly
Deliver both recurring and ad hoc analysis based on evolving business needs
Ensure stakeholders understand and can effectively use reporting tools and outputs


Partner Across the Business


Collaborate closely with Distribution & Transportation leadership, Supply Chain teams, IT, and Data Governance
Gather and refine reporting requirements to deliver meaningful, impactful solutions
Serve as a key bridge between data and the business ensuring alignment and clarity


Solve Complex Problems


Perform advanced analysis to uncover deeper insights
Use business modeling to evaluate potential outcomes and guide decision-making
Proactively identify opportunities to improve processes, performance, and efficiency


Tell the Story Behind the Data


Transform large, complex data sets into clear, compelling narratives
Develop presentation-ready materials for leadership reviews and strategic discussions
Help leaders understand not just the data but the story, impact, and next steps


Drive Innovation & Continuous Improvement


Stay current on analytics tools and emerging best practices
Expand capabilities in reporting, visualization, and modeling
Support a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement


Be a Key Contributor


Provide subject matter expertise on analytics tools and reporting capabilities
Support key initiatives, projects, and cross-functional teams
Help champion change by using data to guide growth and transformation


What You Bring

We're looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and confident working with both data and people someone who enjoys solving problems and influencing outcomes.

Key Skills & Strengths


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with large data sets
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivots, etc.)
Experience with data visualization and analytics tools (Power BI, Alteryx, etc.)
Experience creating calculated expressions (DAX, Calculate fields, Excel Formulas, etc.)
Ability to query using SQL in Databricks
Knowledge of Power Automate and Power Apps
Ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
Strong storytelling and presentation skills
Knowledge of supply chain, inventory, forecasting, or distribution operations (preferred)
Strong organizational and time management abilities
Ability to collaborate, influence, and build relationships across teams
Creative, innovative thinker with a proactive mindset
Ability to navigate change and drive continuous improvement


Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in business, economics, engineering, math, statistics, or a related field
Analytics certifications are a plus
3-5 years of experience in analytics, reporting, or a data-driven environment


What Makes This Opportunity Exciting


You'll work directly with leadership and influence real business decisions
You'll turn data into insights that drive measurable impact across the network
You'll gain exposure to multiple areas of the business and expand your influence
You'll help shape how analytics supports the future of Distribution & Transportation
You'll be part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement


Let's Build What's Next Together

If you're passionate about using data to tell stories, solve problems, and drive meaningful change, this is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

We are an organization that values diversity and encourages all qualified individuals to apply without regard to sex, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, religion, age, marital status (including civil unions), military service, veteran status, pregnancy (including childbirth and related medical conditions), genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, legally recognized disability, domestic violence victim status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Our organization is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities, including applicants. If you have a disability and require assistance in the application process, please email recruiting@adusasc.com

Title: Data Analyst - Enterprise Information Management
Description:
The Data Analyst provides support for both day-to-day operations and new development activities across all electronic systems and interface points. This role requires regular interaction with internal and external stakeholders, engagement with senior management, and the coordination of activities with third-party service providers.

General Responsibilities


Assists with analyzing complex business problems to be solved with automated systems.
Participates in identifying gaps and providing technical expertise in business requirements for system functional specifications and scales new and current systems, processes and procedures in a cost effective manner.
Coordinates the system settings and options; plans and executes unit, integration and acceptance testing to meet business requirements.
May lead cross-functional linked teams to address business or systems issues.
May be client-focused, working in conjunction with Professional Services and Outsourcing functions.


Experience / Qualifications


A university degree required (i.e. Bachelors degree) or equivalent relevant work experience.
Business ethics - Ability to practice and behave consistently and effectively in areas of business practice and conflicts of interest.
Communication - Ability to communicate effectively by getting the right message across to the right people at the right time on a regular basis.
Customer Orientation - Ability to meet customers’ expectations by identifying and anticipating customers’ needs and integrating these into work; ability to develop new added value for customers and act with the customer in mind.
Result orientation - Ability to take steps personally to ensure that mutual goals and performance targets are met within the appropriate timeframe.
Teamwork - Ability to work with others and work synergistically with fellow team-members
Planning and Organizing - Ability to plan, prioritize and organize work and schedules in all areas to meet requirements.
Problem solving - Ability to reach logical conclusions to situations by appropriately analyzing the situation. Balances the benefits, risks and consequences of actions.
Time Management - Ability to assessing your ability to manage your time, and the effectiveness of your team to reach department objectives.
Conflict management - Ability to recognize and work towards a mutually agreeable solution when confronted with conflict.
Resilience - Ability to work under pressure and prioritize.


Base Salary Range:

$64,400.00 - $88,550.00

At Sensata, our employees are the key to our success and growth. We recognize that each individual brings their own unique experience, therefore the base salary range information shown above is a general guideline only. Sensata considers several factors when extending an offer, including, but not limited to, a candidate’s experience and qualifications, as well as internal equity, market and business considerations. In addition to base salary, Sensata offers competitive medical, dental, vision, life and disability insurance plans, along with education reimbursement, wellness programs, a 401(k) retirement plan with Company matching, and a variety of paid time off, such as vacation or flex-time, sick, bereavement, and parental leave. Certain positions are also eligible for short-term incentive and long-term incentive programs.

SmarterTogether


Collaborating at Sensata means working with some of the world’s most talented people in an enriching environment that is constantly pushing towards the next best thing
Employees work across functions, countries and cultures gaining new perspectives through mutual respect and open communication
As OneSensata, we are working together to make things work together

Title: Renewable Cost Analyst
Description:
Wood Mackenzie is the global leader in analytics, insights and proprietary data across the entire energy and natural resources landscape.

For over 50 years our work has guided the decisions of the world’s most influential energy producers, utilities companies, financial institutions and governments.

Now, with the world’s energy system more complex and interconnected than ever before, sector-specific views are no longer enough. That’s why we’ve redefined what’s possible with Intelligence Connected.

By fusing our unparalleled proprietary data with the sharpest analytical minds, all supercharged by Synoptic AI, we deliver a clear, interconnected view of the entire value chain. Our trusted team of 2,700 experts across 30 countries breaks siloes and connects industries, markets and regions across the globe.

This empowers our customers to identify risk sooner, spot opportunities faster and recalibrate strategy with confidence – whether planning days, weeks, months or decades ahead.

Wood Mackenzie

Intelligence Connected

Wood Mackenzie Brand Video

Wood Mackenzie Values


Inclusive – we succeed together
Trusting – we choose to trust each other
Customer committed – we put customers at the heart of our decisions
Future Focused – we accelerate change
Curious – we turn knowledge into action


Role Purpose

Join our Supply Chain team where your analysis directly impacts decisions in the global energy sector. Our team is responsible for developing and managing cutting-edge market intelligence solutions, delivered through our comprehensive suite of software-as-a-service products, and our clients primarily leverage these insights for strategic planning, investment decision making, cost reduction, and risk management in the energy industry.

For this role, we are seeking a talented and versatile renewable cost analyst to support the development, expansion, and enhancement of Wood Mackenzie’s Renewable Asset Cost Modelling (RACM) Solution Suite. You’ll work with a global team and own our renewable system pricing analysis. This role requires subject matter expertise, analytical thinking, and advanced data analysis skills to drive innovation and deliver value to our clients in the renewable sector.

Working with product development, research, consulting, sales, and customer success teams, you'll build a powerful network while transforming complex market data into actionable insights that shape strategy for the renewable industry.

Main Responsibilities


Serve as a subject matter expert on renewable costs to clients. Respond to client inquiries, providing customized solutions, and demonstrating RACM’s value throughout pre- and post-sales interactions.
Analyze and understand ongoing market events influencing capital and O&M costs for renewable assets. For example, how are new tariffs going to impact costs in the US?
Work with Wood Mackenzie researchers across the globe to ensure costs published through research flow into RACM.
Collect, clean, and analyze proprietary datasets to build, refine, and expand renewable data within the RACM product line.
Develop and implement efficient data pipelines and workflows to optimize data ingestion, transformation, and integration processes.
Perform rigorous quality assurance testing to ensure the accuracy and reliability of new features prior to release.
Deploy AI to automate complex data workflows.
Collaborate with engineering, product, and research teams to advance the functionality, scalability, and innovation of the RACM product line, driving the delivery of new features and capabilities.
Contribute to market reports on renewable system costs and technology trends.


About You


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Engineering or a related field.
Experience in the renewables industry, focused on costs and supply chains. Experience in the O&M space is a plus.
A strong understanding of renewable systems and major cost components. Genuine interest in and knowledge of the renewable industry and energy transition.
Strong expertise in data collection, cleansing, and analysis, with a proven ability to build and enhance data-driven products.
Advanced in Microsoft Excel, Python, R, SQL, and AI assistants.
Demonstrated success in collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver innovative, high-impact solutions.
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to clearly translate complex technical concepts for diverse audiences.
Proactive problem solver with excellent organizational and critical-thinking abilities.
Experience in designing and implementing data pipelines and ETL workflows is a plus.


The salary range for this position is $105,000- $120,000, which represents base pay only and does not include short-term incentive compensation or commission. When determining base pay, as part of a final compensation package, we consider several factors such as location, experience, qualifications, and training.

This position does not provide visa sponsorship

Equal Opportunities

We are an equal opportunities employer. This means we are committed to recruiting the best people regardless of their race, colour, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. You can find out more about your rights under the law at www.eeoc.gov

If you are applying for a role and have a physical or mental disability, we will support you with your application or through the hiring process.
